Not the best, but for the price, it can’t be beat. I purchased this dress with the help of others reviews. The fabric of the dress is much nicer than I expected for a $34 dress, it’s really nice. The fabric is thick but it does show panty lines, another thing I dislike; no one needs to know when I have a wedgie. I am 5’ 4” and weigh 210lbs, I ordered the XXL. The bottom half of the dress fits nicely but the top is huge. I know it’s not too noticeable in the photos but it is way too big. I wear a 40 DDD bra size and it’s huge on me. Not only on the front but the back too. It looks sloppy on the back because it’s so big, those are not fat rolls, that’s the loose fabric. I’ve ordered a size down, hopefully it fits better. But overall I am happy with the dress, for that price, you can’t beat it.

Cute! I purchased this for my 40th bday photoshoot. I got it altered because it was too long. I'm 5'3. It served the purpose though. It's not the most amazing dress in the world, but I liked it! I struggled finding a body shaper that was good for it because the few I bought were also visible underneath due to the detailing on them. I ended up going without one, which sucked. It still worked out in the end, but just be aware. I still recommend it if you have a perfect body, don't care about showing body imperfections or if you can find a perfectly smooth body shaper with no bumps, zippers etc.

Unique! I purchased this FQA long formal dress in ink green and size x-large. I purchased this for myself, 5'5" and 189lbs, but I had my daughter model this. My model is 5'9" tall, 200lbs, wears an 18/20, and 36dd. This dress is unique with the one shoulder ruffle. The ink green is so dark, it's difficult to tell it's green. In the south, this would be considered "Charleston green". The material has stretch and is pretty forgiving. The seamwork is excellent, no loose threads. The zipper is hidden and operates well. On my 5'9" daughter, the length works minus an inch or 2 from the floor. A shorter person may need heels. Shapewear/body smoother is recommended to help hide undergarment lines etc. Overall, this is a beautiful, unique formal dress that is a good value at $50

Flattering and gorgeous! I purchased this Miusol sequin lace maxi dress to use as a mother of the groom dress. I purchased the dark green in xx-large based on what I typically wear, which is a size 18. The model is 5'9" and around 200lbs. The fit is true to size and accurate according to their size chart. The material is good quality-the base lace material is thick enough to feel substantial, but lightweight enough that you don't feel the dress is too heavy. The tule is good quality and cut well. All stitching is solidly done-no loose threads. The zipper operates well, but was not needed since neckline is generous-just pulled it over the head. This definitely has a mermaid silhouette, but the tule helps soften the lines and allows some flowiness. The only negative is that the neckline is slightly wide at the back. You can see in the pictures that it's a bit loose. But with hair covering it, it won't show, or I can probably carefully take it in a little. I think this gorgeous dress is a terrific value!

Very flattering for larger bodies, Tight around the arms, Deep Cleavage, Sensory Warning I purchased XXL (the size chart says this is an 18W-20W), my usual dress size. I'm 5'6, 265 lbs. Beneath I am wearing a 46DDD bra, shapewear, and a lace camisole to cover my bust. The dress is extremely flattering. The delicate fabric was delivered without any damage or odd smells. I do take issue with a few minor details: The upper portion of the dress is not very stretchy. I'm a large woman and large women tend to have large upper arms. I understand the intention was for it to be form-fitting, however, there are fabrics out there that would hug my arm without being so tight. It was difficult to adjust the fabric around my arms (was easy to take off though!) The bust is also... very deep. I got a lace camisole to cover up a bit. I'm glad I did, otherwise I would have much more cleavage out which might work for some people but not for meeeee Most important: If you are autistic or struggle with sensory overload please be aware that the skirt fabric might be too much for you. The shapewear under my dress is protecting my midsection from the horrible seam between the top and the skirt touching me but the hem of the skirt is a potential issue after an extended period of wearing it.

Too bad about the "saddle bags" I really like everything about Lee jeans except that no matter which version I buy, they ALWAYS seem to have extra fabric on the outside of the thighs for so-called "saddlebags." Hey! Not all women have these! I hate the little pockets this extra fabric creates--at times I feel like I'm wearing jodhpurs--and in the past I've even tried to sew them off, which has generally made them look dumb in other ways! Otherwise, these jeans are really nice. They have a comfortable degree of stretch, the length is just right (I'm 5'3", 130 lbs & ordered a 6 short), the blue color is pretty, & for the price, the quality is pretty good. I just wish Lee would ditch the extra fabric on the sides--not all women's figures are the same!

I'll buy this is several colors, it's very versatile! 100% polyester, so easy care. I love it!

Great dress for the price! I really like this dress! I needed something semi-dressy with a floral theme for an event on a recent trip to France. This dress was perfect because it is small and easy to pack (it collected only a few wrinkles and they came out with a little Wrinkle Release, followed by showering with the dress hanging in the bathroom). It is VERY forgiving and VERY comfortable. I didn't even need a bra with it due to the cut, so I didn't have to worry about packing a strapless one. I wore it to dinner with heels and this little bolero wrap jacket in the picture, and I was able to wear it out again for a nice lunch with a black sweater over the top, boots, and opaque tights. It's a perfect, versatile dress for travel. The material is lightweight but the underlayer is heavier, so it's not see-through. The only problem I could see is that I'm tall and it was both long in the skirt and in the torso for me--I'm afraid if you were petite, this would swallow you up. Also, though my husband liked it, he started calling it my "curtains" dress because he said that's what the material looks like 😆. Whatever--I was happy in it. It's nicely stretchy and swingy, I didn't have to shimmy into Spanx to wear it, and I could eat cheese and bread without worrying my tummy would pop out. I love it and I will definitely take it with me on future trips because it's so versatile and packs so easily. I washed in cold/delicate and dried flat. For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the XL (I think--there was no size tag when I received the dress).

She is gorgeous! I really like this dress! It is super flattering and very well made. It looks very much like the advertised pictures, so I am quite pleased about that. My one issue is that I wish there was a snap on the neckline to close it up a little. The cut is pretty low for conservative settings like work or church. I ended up having to safety pin the neckline, which is not ideal. A simple sewn-in snap could easily resolve this problem. Otherwise, the dress is great! The fit is fantastic… I followed the size chart and it fit perfectly. The material has some stretch to it, so it hugs the body very well. I did wear shape wear underneath this dress to smooth out the overall look, but the material and construction of the dress were great in hiding any lumps and bumps. The midi length is also spot-on… I’m 5’6” and it fell right below my knees. And I adore the lace sleeves! They really take the dress to the next level and add an interesting element to the overall look. I have quite a few dresses that I mainly wear to work… this one is definitely one of my favorites. It looks so good on and it made me feel good wearing it. I highly recommend for daytime or nighttime; it’s versatile enough to wear throughout the day!

Tronjori Palazzo Pants I really read through the reviews - especially the reviews on those who were 5'10 - 5'11". I'm 5'11" and 165 lbs. I usually buy large in pants to accommodate my hips. Some suggested ordering 1 and/or 2 sizes up. I ordered 2 sizes up (olive) and found the pants to be too large. I also ordered a black pair - one size up size and the fit is fine, but I think I could have ordered a large and would have been okay. However sometimes going with the larger size you may gain a tad more in length because the fabric drapes versus fitting too snugly. The hem of the pants come right at my ankle. I would have liked them a tad longer. I may let the hem out or embellish the hem some way. Nevertheless, the black pair looks great with a pair of strappy sandals with a heel and are comfortable and classy looking.(Addition) I did add some beautiful trim (that I purchased from JoAnn Fabrics) to the black pants to lengthen them. Although some are wearing palazzo pants at the ankle, I prefer to wear mine full length. I am totally pleased with the results.

Great idea, but poor fabric and cut I really wanted to love this dress and thought it would be an easy win, but I'm actually disappointed by it. The dark green is pretty, but the fabric is weird. It's pretty heavy/structured, so it doesn't flow nicely. But then it's also clingy-- I tried the usual tricks to get rid of static cling, but it's annoying to have to treat this like a satin skirt or have it stick to you. The skirt is also not cut like the product photos. Instead of a fit and flare A-line, it's a pretty straight maxi that only widens a little at the bottom. I'm 5'10 and instead of hitting lower/mid-calf like the model, it reaches my ankle bones, so it's also longer than expected. Between the narrower skirt cut and the clingy fabric, the bottom of this dress looks small on me- especially in photos. But it's actually too big and that drives me crazy. It's unnecessarily unflattering. I'm a size 10, ordered a large, actually shrank it a little in the wash, and it still hangs off me like a sack. I love the idea of the wrap ties, but the fabric structure creates big bubbles and ridges in the front that stick out from your body at weird angles. Overall, I'm disappointed by this dress. It's such a great concept and it seems like it should be a pretty universally flattering dress and then it's just not.

Aside from the awkward stitching across the top, this is a nice dress that looks, fits and wears as advertised. The material is of thicker quality (polyester) and stretchy. I ordered the white color in a size medium and feel it's a good fit for my 5'7 - 145lb - 34ddd frame.

Many compliments…great fit I received many compliments on this dress, and it photographs beautifully. The material is not thin, and it has a little bit of weight to it. I’m 5’2” and wore 3.75” heels, purchased a small. Sometimes, I found myself stepping on it. It also kept me warm in cooler temps (60’s) at a evening reception. There is some stretch to it as well. It’s very comfortable to wear. It came wrinkled and I had to steam the dress. Note: Use a steamer, not a hot iron on steam. I accidentally touched the dress with the hot iron, and it melted the dress in a couple spots. It didn’t melt all the way through and with the front ruffle, it hid the spot I melted in the front. I hope I get a chance to wear it again. I purchased a beautiful pearl sash belt on black satin ribbon to wear but it was too dressy for this event. Another way to dress it up and change the look a little.

I bought this over the $100 version I saw this dress on a website several months ago and fell in love, but it was well over $100. I reversed searched the dress on Amazon, and found this one. I could tell from comparing the photos that it wasn’t a re-creation or knock off, they were the EXACT same dresses. The other was just marked up a lot because it was on an aesthetic website. This is super common, so always reverse search your favorite higher priced items!! It no more or less ethical, because they’re all paying the factories the same amount. Onto the dress itself: the dress is BEAUTIFUL in person but I was surprised by how thin the material was. I wouldn’t say it wrinkles super easily, but it definitely does crease easily, as you can see around my hips and under my bust, where the dress would gather. I’m wearing this dress at 5 months pregnant. The corset back helped me look SNATCHED. I really didn’t look pregnant from the front, but belly was on full display when I turned to the side. As I said, the material is very thin, so it’s not going to really hold you in or do a lot of disguising. I’m larger chested and definitely struggled to keep my cleavage from being too exposed, as the the top would kinda flop open. I think a C or D cup would fit this dress size (Small) best. I’m also 5’2 and wearing tall platform heels.

Great deal! I say get the dress a size bigger, fit well. Perfect for any kind of outing and it’s a great price too. The only thing is if you sweat, its very noticeable. Overall nice dress, and comfortable! I’m 5’7 it stops right above the knee so it could be short if you aren't wearing heels o

Perfect ! I searched everywhere for a dress for family maternity pictures. After reading requires i decided to go with this one . The fit was perfect! im 5’9 and 32 weeks pregnant & the length was perfect for a heel. Buy - you won’t regret it!

Loved this dress I searched for months for a burnt orange dress. I found this and loved. I ordered one size up, a 12 and fit perfectly. I typically wear a 10 in dress. I’m 5’5 160lbs 38D for reference. I typically wear large in shirt and medium pant. The back is a corset so adjustments are easy, I could’ve ordered the size 10 and fit perfect too. Pretty good material. The straps can be worn two different ways, we liked with out the straps but didn’t want my girls to fall out lol it did come from china but got to me with in two weeks. The tracking for the shipping showed it bouncing all over NY but that’s because of the international shipping hubs. I love this dress and hope I can wear it again one day.

Obscenely Georgous! I searched high and low for the right dress and as soon as I slipped this dress over my head I knew it was the one. The fabric is very soft and has just the right amount of stretch. It's lacy and delicate but still thick enough not to feel flimsy or see through. I can tell I will be comfortable in it all day and can get married then party all night without wanting to change. The sleeves and neckline are beautiful. I especially love the way it comes up in the front so I don't feel frumpy like I often do in long skirts... yet it still has a long back so it feels fancy. I can't say enough good things about this awesome dress! I'm tempted to order one or two more in different colors just because they're so lovely. I wear a 1x or 2x normally - 220 lbs and 5 foot 7 inches. Ordered a 2x and it's spot on. Buy with confidence!

Nice for the money runs a bit large and I don’t know how to get the wrinkles out I seldom order clothing online because of the fit and this fit quite well. Definitely the sizing is too large in the bust per the measurements that come with it so I specifically ordered a size larger to make sure it fit and I wish I had gotten my normal size as the bust area would still have been a little bit too large. Some of the tailoring on the left side that creates the starburst is a little extra as well versus the pictures of the skinny model in the ad as I have tons of extra material in comparison to her and a size down might have taken away some of that extra material. Overall it’s an amazing dress for the price. I know the price can vary based upon the color and size you choose but I don’t think you’ll find anything close to this dress at any store or online. My only big negative and it’s a big one is these come in a little plastic bag that’s been then folded over four times and I don’t know how to get the wrinkles out it was incredibly wrinkled! Since the garment care instructions say do not steam do not dry clean I honestly do not know how to get all the wrinkles out of it and may not end up being able to wear it at all. I did go through all the reviews searching for the word wrinkle and no one had posted anything about that. The fabric itself has built in spandex is kind of reminiscent of a dry suit for water skiing. Not as thick as like scuba gear but definitely thicker and heavier which then it makes you feel little more protected and it little more covering of imperfections you might have. Lastly as others have stated the him or the bottom of the dress rather has no him. It’s just kind of cut edge and while the length is fine for me while I’m in heels I would think of him might want to survive a bit longer. I am 5’5and 165 and I’m a little more larger in the waist and hips and I wear a 36C cup. I ended up ordering a 14 and I should’ve just stayed with the 12 and I think I would’ve been fine.

It was a hit I selected this dress for a concert I played last night. The first piece we did was “What Was I Made For” from the Barbie movie, so I needed a pink dress! I usually wear a size 12 and ordered the Large. It was like the dress was made for me! So, so many compliments on the dress. I’m 5’6” and it was a little long, but it worked ok. For my granddaughters wedding I sew so I was considering making a dress to wear to my granddaughter’s wedding. But when I saw this one, and the price was good. I’d almost spend that on pattern and fabric. I decided to try it. I was happy it’s well made. I’m 5’8 and it’s long enough too. Not sure about heels though

Not as shiny as it looks I shopped high and low for a dress to wear to my daughter's daytime June wedding and this is the one we chose! I was willing to spend a lot more, but this dress was perfect. I'm 5'4" and 140 lbs and the large was a perfect fit with no alterations. I was worried the chest would sag open with movement but it stayed in place like a champ. Note that the material is not as shiny as the published photos make it look, but that worked for me.

Shoulders Are TooBig I shouldn't have sized up. The XL was too big for 38DD. The shoulders were far too loose; they also look a little cheap. Overall, the dress has some decent weight to it, so not too chintzy, but there are a bunch of finishing issues resulting in untrimmed/loose threads. Most are barely noticeable and could be snipped with steady hands, but this just shows is $36.79 value. I know, you get what you pay for, and if this dress fits you, the quality of the dress won't be too obvious. The navy color is quite nice. Not too dark and not too bright, it's very rich in hue.

Amazing Quality and Fit for Price I simply cannot believe the quality of this dress for less than $60. I am 5’2” 120lbs 34B/32C and I did not have to alter the top of the (size small) dress at all. Fabric is soft and has a good amount of stretch to it. The dress is lined which made it so I could wear a strapless bra and lace panties without seeing underwear lines. (WIN!) I did have to hem it 3.5” (and I was wearing 3.5” heels) so if you are short you will need to do that. The plus side is that the liner is sewn to the dress, so my friend only had to sew one layer. The fabric will catch on stuff, for example it rubbed on the tops of my shoes and got a little pilly, but I mainly bought it to wear for one special day and to look good in photos, and I achieved that! It was so comfortable. The zippered slit is amazing. I had it completely unzipped because it felt sexier, but I got cold later in the evening and it was great to zip it closed. If you do zip halfway, it will stay in place. There is another zipper on the side, not the back, so I didn’t have to ask anyone to help me get dressed or undressed. I felt so elegant and got so many compliments. Can I just say….. THE SLEEVES! Also the cut of the shoulders really highlighted my neckline (yay for feeling sexy without having to show cleavage). I can’t say enough good things about this dress. I love that I didn’t have to panic when it started pouring and my dress ended up getting a little muddy in the garden, because it wasn’t $3,000. Couldn’t be happier with this dress option for my COVID wedding.

Very pretty! True to size I sized up 1 size just in case and it fits pretty perfect. I feel like the 20W would have but the 22W gave me just enough wiggle room without being too big. The dress is long but I’m 5’4.5” and it hits the floor but I plan to wear heels so it will be fine. I’ll add pictures when I wear it to a wedding in march. Great buy!

Soooo gorgeous I sized up and glad I did because it might’ve sat weird on my tummy if I hadn’t. I’m 5’4 and it didn’t go all the way down which I would have wanted but still so beautiful. Good quality.

Beautiful but…. I sized up like the reviews said. I’m normally a 10/12 and I ordered a 14. I was concerned about the arms before purchasing since I have bigger arms but the stretch was perfect which made the arms fit excellent. The only downside to this dress is the sequence gets stuck on each other under the arms. Lost a few sequences just trying it on. I have a feeling I’m gonna get frustrated all night with that. Otherwise it’s a beautiful and flattering dress. I’m 5’3 and the length is perfect with some heels.

It's pretty, but.... I so wanted to love this dress, I had originally bought it for a pinup style truck show. Unfortunately I don't know if I will be wearing it or not. The fabric is very soft and stretchy, although it's also clingy and the piece underneath the bust is so tight it's almost uncomfortable. It has zero stretch and that's where you need it to stretch I tried sitting down in it and I almost ripped it that's how tight it is. I bought this in the largest size they had which was a US 26, or a UK 30. I can honestly tell you that this dress is not a US size 26, it might be maybe a US size 24. I read all the reviews and I looked at all the pictures so I figured I'd be fine buying it in the largest size. The dress does have pockets which is awesome except they sit right in one of my heaviest spots and I think it makes me look even heavier because they protrude out. Maybe I can fix it by buying a petticoat and undoing the waist and putting elastic in it and resewing it together. I am 5'8 ,290 lb the dress sits just below my knee which is a very nice length. I have a very large chest I am a 50 G, and I have shoulders like a linebacker. I'm hoping that maybe this review will help some other girls fitting my shape as well, which is basically an upside down pear , heavy on the top and small past belly button.

Perfect for any formal occasion! I so wish I got more/better pictures in this dress! I wore it for New Year’s and it was perfect! This dress is perfect for any formal occasion and it’s comfortable and easy to move in! (Cause you know I had to dance into the new year) The color of the dress was beautiful, just as advertised! For reference I’m 205 lbs and 5’7 and I ordered this dress in an X-Large. I appreciated that it showed a little cleavage but not too much, and the long sleeves kept me warm since I live where the weather is on the colder side.

Confusing to get on I spent a good 5 mins trying to figure out how to get this on. It's a beautiful dress once you do get it on. I personally will need to use tape to hold everything in place. I haven't seen a bra that would work with the way this is designed. It's very sexy, especially with the slit, and will for sure turn heads. I got a small and it fits fine being 5'7" and 163 pounds.

Great for busty gals! I spent months looking for a dress for my sisters wedding that fit my postpartum/nursing body. This fit great. Plenty of room in the bust (I'm a 40h) and didn't require a strapless bra. The material was thick and felt a lot more expensive than it was. I got lots of compliments! For reference I'm 5'4 and 210lbs.

Belle of the Ball I spotted this dress as I searched for an outfit to wear for formal occasions. What caught my eye was the lovely lace over the beige underskirt and the empire waist cut. I'm only 5'2" in height and apple shaped, so most formal wear needs massive alterations to fit me. Not so with this wonderful dress! The length came down to my ankles giving me a full length without worry of tripping on my heels. The waist was a true empire style which fits my apple shape perfectly. The black upper part of the dress fit well except for the wrap area. It was loose on me due to my not being quite so busty. However, I wore a comfortable black sports bra under it which gave me the support and modesty I prefer and seamlessly worked with the upper black fabric of the dress. While the waist was not stretchy, I followed the size directions and ended up with a perfect fit. I sat, danced, and hung out at the party in total comfort while I received many compliments on my attire.I am giving serious consideration to purchasing a second dress although I only attend a few formal events each year. All three versions are beautiful and unique. If you are like me, short and apple shaped, I would recommend this dress to you.

Cute dress but has spots...? I stand at 5 feet 1 inch, typically wearing a size 8 in dresses. Consequently, I opted for a medium and it fits like a glove. However, due to my height, the dress falls to about mid-calf, which I find less flattering. I'm contemplating alterations to raise it to a more preferable mid-thigh length. Besides that, the dress is undeniably charming. Regrettably, it arrived with what appears to be almost imperceptible mold spots. While they're not glaringly obvious when I wear it, I'm concerned someone might notice them. I'm hopeful that they can be removed.

Great look and quality. Low V line and long dress I struggle a lot to find dresses since I’m really curvy. This one actually went very well with me. Super low V neck line that I have to alter. It’s also very long. I’m 5’5 and about 160 lbs and I ordered a size 10. Great price, would recommend.

Mixed feelings about this one… I think the construction of this garment is good, but I have some reservations about it overall. The buttons are ok, but are very yellow gold and read a bit “cheap”. The heavier tweed-like fabric is nice, but it might feel a bit stiff for some. Definitely remember to size UP! In department stores I am usually a medium. On Amazon I am a large. With this garment, I had to re-order and get an XL. I am by no means broad-shouldered, but I do have a larger bust (36DD) and the large was just too tight in the shoulders and chest overall. Now that I’ve ordered an XL, I feel the fit is much more comfortable, but now the waist looks a bit boxy and frumpy. I wish the garment was more tailored for a woman’s natural curves, but it seems more boxy on my frame than I would like. I guess it’s back to the drawing board (or should I say, “back to the Amazon cart”??) for me. This garment will probably fit better on someone with a leaner, more athletic frame than my hourglass one. (Side note: in natural light, the ‘brown’ looks more warm brown (ie: chocolate) then cool brown (ie: khaki). I noticed my photos made the same jacket look different shades in different lighting.)

I think this blouse is cute and classy! The blouse is well made, with seams nicely finished. The top has two layers; the one closest to the skin is very comfortable, and the outer layer fabric has a little more texture. This top is not too low cut with a neckline that can work with different necklaces. The ruffle bell sleeves are on trend, allowing the blouse to be worn multiple seasons. I am 5 ft 3 in, 135 lb, 32D bra, (age 60) and wear a size 10 dress or jeans. Based on the size chart, I selected size L. In these photos, I am also wearing a flesh colored camisole, but I did not think the double layer of fabric to be too sheer. While most of the wrinkles from shipping quickly disappeared after hanging, I used a fabric steamer to give it a smooth finish. Afterwards, I tried to make the fabric wrinkle, but it stayed wrinkle free! (I have not washed this top yet.) I look forward to wearing it!!

Nice simple dress I think this dress has a lot to offer but also has some drawbacks. The top of the dress is lined but the bottom isn't so dark undergarments are clearly visible. For this photo, I was wearing a slip which seemed to do a great job of covering everything up. I didn't notice a huge difference in sizing between the small and the medium. The navy dress is a small and the pink dress is a medium. Both fit decent but the medium was a bit more comfortable for my arms. For reference, I am 5'9" and about 132 pounds. I typically wear a size 4/6 in dresses (I have a small waist, long torso, and larger hips). Even given my height, this dress is pretty long and hits below the knee. There are no loops for the ribbon so it can be worn with or without it. This was just a try on, and I haven't worn or washed the dresses so I can't speak to the longevity. I can say this is a lovely, modest dress at a great price. It can easily be dressed up or down, and I'm looking forward to pairing it with tights and a cardigan to wear all winter.

The buttons could be better secured I think this is a cute look… nothing too overwhelming or very different about it. However, I was disappointed that a button had popped off during the day and I didn’t even know it until I looked in the mirror! There definitely needs to be more stitching to keep the buttons in place for a long time. Otherwise, I don’t have any major complaints. The fit is pretty good… I followed the size chart and didn’t have any issues with fit. Also, it’s listed as a mini dress, but the length actually fell to right above my knee- which is great for me because it made the dress more appropriate to wear to work (I’m 5’6”). The material is really soft and comfy! I love the ribbed knit… it was very cozy to wear in fall temperatures. And the pleating just gives the overall look a little dimension. I also liked the fact that the dress was form fitting at the top and the skirt flared at the bottom; it created a very flattering silhouette. And I do like the button-up design at the top, especially how the white buttons and lines play off of the brown dress and really pop. I just wish the buttons wouldn’t pop off the dress is all! Overall, I think this is a fine purchase. If you have some sewing skills and can reinforce the sewing on these buttons, I’d highly recommend you do so before you wear it. Fortunately, it was just the top button that popped off for me… a button in the middle or at the bottom could have really caused a problem.

Things to know: Color, fit, quality I thought it looked lovely online and a good value so I took a chance. Here is the scoop. The product arrived in about 2.5 weeks from overseas, so shipping was on the shorter window in the ETA vs. the longer time, which was a plus. Another plus was that this garment was much nicer quality that I expected for such a low price point. The sequin works was flawless. This garment didn't feel like a cheap costume, it felt like a "real dress" if that makes sense.I chose to RETURN the dress for the following reasons:The color I ordered was NOT close to the picture. I know you can't always take the photo for truth, but the description says "gold and apricot" I was thinking it may be a peachy or cream color with a hint of pink.... nope.. straight up YELLOW. It's a pale yellow much like a banana creme pie. I wanted something vintage looking and this makes me feel like Belle from beauty and the beast.The second reason I'm returning it is because of the fit. I ordered a medium and it was on a bigger side of a medium. (I am 5'4" 145lbs and size D bust). The dress didn't have much curve to it. The front and back panel of the dress was exactly the same (think of a floor length v-neck tee-shirt. Two FLAT panels sewn together at the sides). I'm no expert but a female body differs slightly from the front to the back. It wasn't flattering at all. My butt pushed the back of the dress out and it make the fabric at my back loose, and my bust pushed the front of my dress out to make the fabric by my stomach loose. I have a generally nice shape and I looked like a sack of bananas in this dress.Notes: If you are more of a barrel shape or very rectangular shape with not a lot of curves I think this item will be flattering on your body type. Also if you think you are between a M & L take a chance on the M because I thought it was a very generous medium and ran a little big.

Buying Clothing on Amazon is usually risky I took a risk buying this dress at the last minute for a "Purple & White Gala" I was invited to. I had a completely different dress I was going to wear until the person told me 2 days before the event that we should wear Purple. I loved the style of this dress and recently lost 44lbs, so I thought I'd show off. For a point of reference, I'm 6ft tall and 257lbs. Normally, I'd go for a size 18/20 in dresses and still size 20 jeans because I've got junk in my trunk and I've gotten used to that and am scared to go down in size for fear it'll be too tight and I'd barely be able to breathe or sit without ripping it. I tried to go for the XXL here but I wouldn't get it in time for the event. I sat here and read every single review and looked at every single picture in hopes I'd see at least one lady with my height and weight/shape so I could order accordingly. Some were hard to gage with just pics but no reference points, some gave reference points but were not like mine, some had no pics at all. I needed to see where the dress would fall for my height, how it would fit everywhere else. I read every single question/answer in hopes of seeing someone with my stats but no luck. I had to go off a mixture of people to make a decision as to whether I should go for the 14/16 XL or not because I could get the dress next day. After reading the reviews and seeing pics of all you beautiful ladies, I decided to go for the XL and I'm SO glad I did! It fit PERFECTLY everywhere!! So for that lady who might look like me and can't decide..get the XL and some Spanx! The dress stretches nicely and hugs ALL the curves. It's extremely flattering! Head turns, stares and compliments all night! Do it!!

Beautiful dress, check sizing! I took my measures with their chart and came up to a size 6. I ordered 6 even though I’m usually 4 (I’m 4’11”, 110 lbs). Came way too big in every part, boobs, straps, waist. Didn’t have time to return + get another one, so I just took it to alterations, made the straps shorter, took the waist in, made the length shorter. It was way better but the straps were too broad (maybe it was because the size was too big) but it made me look older, so I made a quick alteration at home and made the a bit thinner. I was very happy with the final result. I would advise to get 2 sizes and return what doesn’t fit! Happy with the purchase tho. Got a lot of compliments!

My first thought was that it was just an odd feeling and my finger slipped off the material, but nope, straight through it! I’m not sure if they were already torn and I just didn’t notice because of the tags or something, or if the material was really that weak?! I’m super sad because they were really cute! Dress does not disappoint. It looks and feels great! I used this dress for my 30 yr. Vow renewal. I used the info from the previous posts and photos to help me make my decision. Marilyn Dress I used this dress for my Marilyn Halloween costume. Moved to bow to the back, added a thick lining (cotton-poly stretch twill fabric), and sewed a ruffle and rhinestone trim on the top. It runs a little large. I'm 5' 1", 130 lbs, 32DDD, and it was about a size bigger than I expected, even on top. I'm normally about an 8-10 on the top, and a 2-4 around my hips. I got a medium. The color photographs beautifully! It is also EXTREMELY long! I wore super high heels, with a 1.5" platform and 5" stiletto, and I still needed to pin up the back to walk around trick or treating with my kiddos. Also, unless you have six pack abs (or close), you're going to want to think seriously about some very smooth yet supportive shapewear to go with this dress. As others have mentioned, the fabric shows all the muffin top and FUPA you've got (and then some). I solved it with a strapless shapewear slip and that THICK lining I mentioned above, but that's a lot of work, and some extra expense and time too. Hard to beat $32 though, if you're willing to put some extra work into this dress, go for it!

Beautiful Dress I used this dress for my maternity photoshoot and it was PERFECT! The material was super stretchy super soft and the dress was just gorgeous! Quality was great! I’m 5’6” and weight about 220 I was 38 weeks pregnant.

Beautiful meets quality. I used this dress in white as my wedding dress. I love the lightness of the material. It was absolutely perfect for our summer wedding! It's a very flattering dress, cut very well, with the right amount of stretch. I am 5'5.5", 185 lbs, and wore a shaper underneath and fit beautifully. I love the neckline, and I'm not really into going sleeveless, but this dress made me feel so beautiful! The lace is so soft and didn't snag on anything. I was planning on changing into other clothes after our ceremony and pictures, and because of how wonderful this dress is, I never changed out of it (until we made it home for the night). I received so many compliments on this dress and highly recommended it to my friends. I also have this same dress in the coral color and can't wait to wear it when we have our little getaway. I am very pleased with the quality of this dress. ❤️ And you can't beat the price.

Cute jacket elevates work outfit I wanted a lightweight but structured jacket to wear with my work clothes on business travel; a normal business jacket would be too hot for the area I was visiting. I was hoping to find something that had lapels, but I ran across this and bought it instead. The straight lines provide some of the structured look I wanted, and it was definitely lightweight. It's not business-suit serious, but it still worked for my needs. I'm 5,7 and 195 pounds, with my weight distributed mostly on top. I like shirts looser and normally buy XL, but bought the Large based on the Amazon suggestion. It fit comfortably except in the upper arm area. There is some stretch but I still I felt it was too tight when worn over a shirt. I returned it for an XL and am much happier. I recommend buying this cute cover up.

Beautiful fabric and color but need to alter, too large I wanted to look like the 50s for my company’s Christmas party. I got the green dress size small. I’m 5’7” and 110 lbs. the dress was too large at the waist and bust. The band was too wide. I took in 4 inches from the armpit to the waist, and reduced the band from 4 inches to 2. It fit much much better. I bought black full length petticoat to make the dress look fuller. After all the alterations, I showed up to the event with hair, make up, and this dress looking like a million bucks! Overall, I was happy with the style, the fabric, and color but I wished they make extra extra small for a petite person like me! If you know how to sew, it’s not hard to alter.

It's a No For Me I wanted to love this dress but it has some issues: 1. Bad chemical smell 2. Shorter than the length listed on the size chart (the dress I received is only 55" but should be 59") 3. Hangs really weird, the stiff netting underneath gives you a lumpy/hippy silhouette 4. You will leave a trail of sequins everywhere 5. No pockets as stated in the title 6. Cheap looking / unfinished straps, looks like they belong on a swimsuit I ordered this in a size 6 with intentions to alter it to my size of 0/2 but I found that if I pulled the straps tight enough I could wear as is if needed. The back just wouldn't be as open. The dress has a nice weight to it. It's not light or flimsy feeling. The bust is slightly big but nothing too noticeable. The length is definitely too short. If this dress was the correct length I may have helped but with it being too short and the weird shaping of the netting underneath, this dress does not look good on me at all. I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 32 A/B-25-34 for reference. After altering I may put some tulle underneath to give it some added length.

Style wasn’t right for my shorter frame I wanted to love this dress, because it looked awesome on the model, but unfortunately I’m only 5’3” and short waisted, so the cut was off for my torso & the waist band made me look boxy, as well as the length in back was a little long for me. Cute party dress that requires an hourglass figure. I wanted to love this dress, but it didn't fit my shape. This dress is made for more of an hourglass figure and someone in their teens or 20s because there is no liner and it's not very forgiving for different body shapes. I'm 5'6", 110 lbs, 34B, and I ordered a small. I didn't fill out the chest area enough and the waist was baggy on me, while the hips fit fine. I may try an XS and see if that works. The dress is very sexy, and I love the color and simple design. The material has a nice level of stretch to it. It's 90% Polyester and 10% Spandex. The material is very shiny like satin. It has a zipper in the back, and I love the slit in the front. I wish this dress fit me properly!! Not sure if I would recommend this dress, it's a hit or miss.

Shorter than advertised, horrible zipper I wanted to try something different to see the overall lol and fit. But this dress has a zipper that doesn’t lie flat and the dress is shorter than expected. I’m only 5’-6”. It does fit true to size…just need the appropriate undergarments (garment tape and boob tape, plus a shaper) to pull off the look, but it’s not wearable due to the zipper. It’s not the best quality, but the satin is thick and smooth. The overall design would have looked good if the dress was long as advertised. The long pieces have to be tied first before draping, especially if you have a larger/heavier bust line. The tying will help keep the girls in place.

Will Not Be Disappointed. I wanted to wait till my banquet to write a review. Beautiful peacock color 2 piece dress w/jacket I wanted until a week before I needed a dress to wear to a friend’s wedding to find one. The 1st of a different style was just ugly on me; the 2nd failed to ship, which left me with the need for a 1 day Prime shipping. Peacock was the only color in my size available that quickly. It arrived as expected in a large box. Inside, the dress was on a hanger inside a plastic bag, but in a heap. I held my breath as I unpackaged it fearing it would be wrinkled or not fit. The wedding is tomorrow! I was overjoyed to see there were NO WRINKLES at all and the color is beautiful. Now for the fit…after I ordered this dress I read there are differing views regarding fit. I wear a medium in tops, but my hips are an issue with a sheath style dress. Amazon recommended a Large, so that’s what I ordered. I do believe I could have worn a Medium, but do not know that for sure. The large fits well, but loosely from the bust down, incredibly soft and comfortable. will not have to worry about needing shapewear or that my love handles will be visible. I don’t have the body for a tight fit. I wear a 36D bra, and the arm openings are ample enough for my fit without any undergarments showing or puckering. There is a minimum amount of beading 2” wide around the neck front & back. The lightweight sheer jacket is a very elegant touch embellished with the same 2” beading at the cuff of the 3/4 length sheer sleeve. I love that it does not close or overlap in the front. For me it drapes over my breast and hangs loosely in the back. I lucked out in the nick of time! For reference I am 5’3”, 155 lbs. this dress, as shown in photos, is just below my knees. I paired with navy sandals and think they will do. I highly recommend this dress set. It was a little more than I wanted to pay at $90, but I had to because I put off the inevitable. I’m hoping to get several events knocked out with this set!

I was “encouraged” to wear a certain color for a wedding as a guest. This was one of my choices and I’m glad I got it thanks to all the photos and reviews I read from others. I am under 5 feet and about 144 in weight with a D cup size. Because I’m short/petite in height, I made alterations to make the dress shorter to elongate my legs at ankle height. I also had to alter the back zipper because when you closed it, it left a little weird “space hump”. But overall dress was comfortable and flowed really nicely. I wore a black short spandex underneath so you couldn’t see through (got it here in Amazon too). Lots of compliments from all ages in women and one gay guy. Side note- stay away from babies, they think it’s pretty too so they’ll be picking on the material. I say get it, and worst case- you return.

Easy to wear, comfortable, NOT see through I was a bridesmaid and the bride sent us a link to these dresses. Mine was velvet the other girls had the normal fabric. The dress was great. We all had it styled differently and they were perfect. We all ranged from size 4 to a size 26, from 5'3(me) to 5'9 and we all looked great! I love that it came with a bandeau bra/cover(wish it had silicone so it wouldn't slide) I used cakes and they were grippy enough that I didnt have it moving. None of the other girls used the bandeau. The regular fabric wrinkled pretty badly while traveling but came right out with a steamer. I an a size 13, I got the regular size, Im curvy, 48/36/46, 5'3, 220lbs. The dress was very high quality, raw edges but I liked them and when its styled they dont look bad at all(I was worried about that). Easy to move, not itchy, thick enough that it is NOT sheer! Great on so many body types and sizes. The best bridesmaid dress I have had to wear. Honestly, I think I will actually wear this one again!

Since it's a jersey stretch fabric it does slip so I used some safety pins to hold in place the areas I didn't want to be exposed for my peace of mind. It was very long. I'm 5'8 and it was well past my feet. If I was going to be on one level I would have opted for higher heels but since I had stairs I did a low level heel, then I tied two knots in the skirt. The knots were parallel to reach other in the front. This left my feet exposed in the front door safety on the stairs but have the dress a train like effect for the back. Many said it have the dress a "Grecian" feel or I was giving "Athenia" vibes. Maybe if I had styled the top differently but I still really appreciated the compliment. What I like the most is I can wear this over and over styled differently for different functions with different jewelry and shoes and I'm good to go. I just wish it had pockets.

A little fussy but worth it I was able to wear this to a very important event and was able to coordinate with him. It's very comfortable to wear. That's once you have it styled in the way you're going to wear it and then secured. Very Kind Customer Service I was about to give up on this dress but customer service was so kind and they addressed my concerns. It takes a bit to understand how to use the corset, in my case I just had a friend help me, on my own I wouldn’t have been able to wear it. I’m 5’1 and it was long, I didn’t have enough time to get it altered but you definitely can message them and give them your measurements. :)

Holy Cow! Effortless Elegance! I was absolutely blown away by this gown. I purchased it on a whim just to add a bit of drama to my closet and have absolutely no regrets. The dress is made out of a stretch glitter polyester and is very forgiving in the sizing. It has a mesh panel trimmed with black lace, a keyhole back, and a waist cinching ruching detail. It has a mermaid silhouette that clings to the hips and flares out at the feet and is so so flattering. Following the sizing chart I decided to get a size XXXL because I have large hips and a 38 inch waist. For reference I usually wear a size 14 to 16. I found that the 3XL fit well, perhaps a little bit loose in the bust but fit comfortably everywhere else. One of my favorite features of the dress is the adjustable slit, it actually has a zipper so you can be as modest or as revealing as you would like. I feel so effortlessly elegant in this gown. It's very comfortable and I could see wearing it all night long, no I just need to find a place to wear it!

I LOVED IT I was actually in a pinch when I bought this dress. I ordered a custom fit one and it was a complete disaster and I was 2 weeks away from my wedding. I had to choose something with prime shipping and this was one of my 2 options. It may not be perfect for everyone, but it was perfect for me. I wanted a side split and this one gives you the option to zip it up if you don’t want the split. My only issue was that it was slightly long, but that’s normal for me bc I’m only 5’2”. I just whipped out my sewing machine and hemmed it up a little. No one could tell bc the lace on the outside of the dress. It was so beautiful and SO comfortable. I was able to wear a normal bra and just pulled the straps down with the dress. The dress had a tight band on the top that held my bra straps in place. You can wear underwear, but I had to ditch mine bc I wore the wrong color and it was visible through the dress 🤦🏼‍♀️ That custom dress being messed up was a huge blessing in disguise. I loved this dress so much. I never would have thought to purchase my dress on Amazon, and for this cheap! 5 stars from me!!!

Does the job! I was afraid about getting a dress from Amazon so last minute for a wedding in England in the middle of Spring…but tbh I got so many compliments on my dress. I didn’t feel it was that cute or flattering bc my boobs are big and I ordered a size too big…but that green truly looks good on me and most likely any super pale woman like me. It’s really long and mad stretchy / comfortable. I’m happy I got it, make sure you get your right size. I think I ordered XL, I was 195lbs, and I’m 5’3”, 38DDD.

Flattering - nice fit I was afraid it wouldn’t fit right, but it fits really well! It’s not too tight in the butt or thighs and it’s not too loose on the top. The fabric stretches well. The length works well for shorter women. I’m 5’4” and the length is good (although it might have been better if it were a little longer if I wear high heels with it). I washed it in the washing machine and laid it flat to dry as instructed. There were a few small wrinkles but nothing too crazy. I tried to lessen them with a steam iron. I like that this can be dressy without being over the top dressy. Nice addition to the wardrobe. Great value.

Hangs in a flattering way and is super comfy; many can size down I was afraid this would be like a paper sack, and it might be hard to tell from the picture, but it hangs and moves in a flattering way on my body. I love that it's flattering without being tight or immodest. I can't wait to wear this out and about now. I was also worried the fabric wouldn't be nice, but it is a good poly spandex type, which not everyone loves but I find versatile. It's a lot better than some other poly blends in my opinion. For reference, I'm 5' 5.5" and currently about 137 lbs. According to the size chart, for my bust (40"), I should have gotten a large, but the medium feels nice on me (and maybe even the small would have worked). I tried this on with some wedge/platform espadrilles (prob 3" heel), and if I wear those, I won't need to hem this. I would need to hem if I planned to always wear this with flats.

Vivid colors for a fun look I was afraid to order this as I’m just 5’1” and most every regular maxi dress is too long. But I’m so glad I did! The dress is about 3” above my ankle and the sleeves aren’t too long. I’m an active 76 year old, weigh 140, and am a 34D - this fits perfectly and the colors are as vivid as pictured. I’ll steam the wrinkles from packaging and take the dress on our next cruise.

BUY IT I was alil unsure about this dress at first. But it was a totally hit!!!! The color look phenomenal against my chocolate skin! I thought it would make me look frumpy because I don’t usually wear a dress that was this flowy from top to bottom…AND IT DID NOT! It sat and flowed in all the right places!!! All night the compliments were rolling In!! And when I posted my pic on social media I had so many ask me where I got this dress from…. AMAZONNNNNNN!!! And my absolute favorite I do not like to iron anything! I put this on straight out the dryer and walked out the door ready for the evening!! LOVE LOVE LOVE this dress!!! I order a small! I’m 5’3 170lbs

What a beautiful gown! I was all set on one gown 6 months before my son's wedding. A week before I tried it on...and I didn't like it. Showed too much arm and just didn't look dressy enough. I was on a mission to get a dress and get one fast. And one that would match the silver shoes I had already bought! I went to every shop, mall, etc. and then turned to Amazon. I ordered a few gowns, tried them on, and sent photos to my SILs and friends for a final verdict. This dress was the winner in more ways than the voting. It was absolutely perfect for an outdoor wedding in early June and I received so many compliments. I even love the photos of me in the dress - a feat in and of itself. I did not think I was going to like the blush material beneath the sequined navy blue, but it looked awesome! The dress was perfect on my 5'7" in frame and just reached the floor with 2 inch heels. When I tell this story, people are shocked that I was fortunate enough to find a dress days before my son's wedding AND that I got it from Amazon! LOL I highly recommend this beautiful gown.
So comfy! I was amazed at how well this fit and how comfortable and flattering it is. Im 5'6, 3XL, it hits the ground just right and the material is super soft.

TALL GIRLS SIZE UP FOR LENGTH I was apprehensive ordering because I’m 5’9 and I always have trouble finding pants that fit in the waist and go down the full length of my legs. I’m a size 28Waist/36Length so I decided to play it safe and order the large and belt if the top was too big. The length stopped right at my ankles after I wore the pants as low-rise on my hips. They got a ton of compliments however and were very comfortable to wear. So if you want the pants to fit in the waist and reach the length of your legs these might not be the best choice for tall girls, but if you’re fine wearing them low waisted they work perfectly.

I felt simply classy lady. I was at a black and white ball and it was just right for me, although I had to get it taken up cause I'm 5'1' and I don't like showing my arms, and I like shimmering things and it had just enough sparkle in it. I loved this dress.

Loved it! This color was beautiful. I was attending a beach wedding and ordered multiple dresses, and this one was the winner! Shopping online can be tricky, so I always rely on reviews for sizing guidance. Unfortunately not everyone does this! But for reference, I’m 5’4”, around 157-160 pounds, with a size 36D chest, and the Medium fit perfectly. I was planning to wear a strapless bra but found that I didn’t need to. Instead, I used the padding from a bathing suit top, and it worked great. This dress was comfortable and flattering, making it perfect for the occasion!

THIS DRESS SLAYED I was attending a black tie event and needed a dress. This was the perfect length (I’m 5’6’’). It came to the top of my feet. I had first gotten the 24, but my back is a bit wider, so I got the 26 and it fit perfectly. I got compliments all night.

Arrived damaged, but don't have time for a replacement I was between sizes, and really should have gotten a medium, but I don't trust that it would be delivered by the 15th for my gender reveal party since the first order took over two weeks. I'm 5'3" and the back of the dress is maybe half an inch off the floor in bare feet. Sleeve length is perfect. Bump friendly for the small bump I have so far, but I would definitely size up if you are past 16-20 weeks. Good coverage on the chest for a faux wrap dress. It also has a plastic snap sewed onto the bust area, but I will have to take it off because it is too high up and doesn't look natural when fastened. The big issue, shown in pictures, is that it arrived with holes by the tag! Again, I don't have time to replace it before my event, so I'll need to sew it somehow. It's disappointing to pay this much for a dress for one event, only for it to arrive with a hole.

user_comment: I was beyond grateful that this last minute snag worked out so well. I did not even try it on until the day of the event. It fit so well, more than I can say in words. It has all of the right areas fitted in the right ways that makes your body flattering instead of all odd like, well mine. I have the hardest time finding nice things being mid-thirties and mom-bod. I am tall 5'11 and I wore an xl, receiving compliments the entire day! I did not even need a body shaper, everything fir perfectly and feeling comfortable during a large event that lasts a long time, is important. Highly recommend.

Beautiful Dress!! I was blown away with the quality of this beautiful dress I purchased for a wedding this summer. The material is 60% Cotton, 35% Polyester, 5% Spandex. I washed it on the hand wash cycle & immediately took it out of the washer & hung it to dry. There were a couple wrinkles but in the drying process they faded. The material is lightweight & definitely sheer. The sparkle around the waist is gorgeous. It fit perfectly around my bust which is 36C. In the picture I was 160lbs & I’m 5’6”. I did begin the ordering process at a size 12 then 10 but both were too big, finally a size 8- shockingly to me fit just right. I hope that helps in ordering your size. I feel the sizing chart isn’t accurate & would order down based on my measurements at the time. I never thought I’d be fitting in a size 8 dress but as you see it fit well. A lot of my weight is in my buttocks rather than stomach. I think that was the deciding factor. The dress is extremely flattering. It zippers in the back so you definitely need help getting it on. It fell just below my knees. Truly the perfect dress for a summer in NY. It was 80 degrees that day but windy & I felt very comfortable wearing it. There isn’t much stretch to the material though. It fit perfectly in my arm pit area as it wasn’t too tight. I look at it & can’t believe I found this beauty on Amazon. It was a gem to have & a cherished day. I hope my review helps you in finding the right dress. Genuine quality for what I paid. I highly recommend!!

Belle of the Ball! I was complimented all night with this gold dress. The tie up back was perfect for size adjustments. Also, I'm 5'11 and 200 pounds, so it's hard to find a good dress that's long enough. I needed it quicker than it would ship, and customer service got right back to me and shipped it quicker. Could not be happier with it.

It's a win I was concerned about the fit and cut, but my daughter REALLY wanted it for a pageant event. I am pleasantly impressed with the dress. She is 11 years old and wearing a size 0 and it is laced as tightly as it will go and I think it fits perfectly. I was afraid it would be too short, but on a 5'2" girl, the length is appropriate. The fabric is comfortable. There is about a 2" hemline on each skirt tier that has a stiffener inside it to keep it's fluffy shape. The straps are adjustable so if you're on the shorter torso side you can lift it up. It is super sparkly in real life! The sequins are not super heavy on it, there is space between them, but they are shiny enough to make up for that.

Just what I needed I was definitely surprised! I opened the package and expected to hate it, it felt heavy and seemed like it was going to be too big. I put it on and it fit great!! Its adjustable so you can tie it as tight or loose as needed. Im 5’2 and 130 and ordered a small. The length stopped right at the floor and I’ll be wearing heels with it which is perfect. If you’re creative like me you can even wrap it the opposite way to make the slit go on the opposite side if desired!! Highly impressed

The best impulse buy ever! I was determined not to buy a new dress for a company event and then three days out, ordered this one a bit impulsively. I had lost some weight and advanced into my sixties since last we had gathered and thought, “Oh, what the heck. Let’s see if I can pull this off!” The dress arrived the next day and I anxiously tried it on. Amazing. I modeled it for my husband and then asked him how much he thought I had paid for it. He gave me his conservative guess of “over $100”. Nope. I wore it to the event yesterday and have never gotten so many compliments- even from people I don’t know. Best morale booster ever! P.S. I wear a 36D bra and went bold with a size small. Fit me like a glove!

Huge I was encouraged when I opened it & found the fabric to be very nice & heavier than expected. When I tried it on I thought I must have accidentally ordered XL, but no. It is a medium & what I normally wear. I'm 5'2 & 127 lbs. I will need to return it.

Nice light t-shirt I was excited to receive this T-shirt as it appeared to be a fairly nice length (I am 5' 10"). I found was that it kicked out oddly at the hip, but is a little snug up top and neckline very high.

Too big I was excited to wear this dress to a wedding, but thank goodness I had a backup option, I’m 5’8 and 120 lbs and this dress was really baggy on me around the waist and stomach area and not flattering at all. I think if they had an extra small it would have worked.

A tall person's dream! I was excited when I saw this dress, nervous after I read the reviews, and now back to excited to wear this to a friend's wedding! I am 5'10 and ~200 lbs, and I ordered the large because I'm not very busty (36B) and didn't want to have too much fabric there. I will say - it's perfect! The waist has some stretch to it, probably within one size if you're normally a US women's 12/14 it should work for you! The silhouette is very flattering, and the black material isn't sheer at all! Obviously the tulle is :) I know many of the reviews mention how long it is but for a tall person... this dress is heaven! Love it so much!

Not at all like pictured I was expecting more of a chiffon feel than a tulle feel, so that is my fault. It says tulle in the description. The picture makes it look soft and flows but it is scratchy and quite firm. Unfortunately it was torn on arrival in the neck area, just under the seam. I had to put it on over my head and the zipper stopped at my waist. I am 5'4 and 195lbs. According to my body measurements and the dress measurements, it should have fit.

The fabric is nice and does not look or feel cheep. Color was spot on. Fit true to size. I will be wearing to a wedding in April.

THE FIT I was extremely skeptical 🤨 but LADIES this dress made me feel SeXy! I like the layers because it sort of hid my belly fat. I’m 5ft 168Ibs 36C, There was some stretch in the dress, I bought an Medium.

SO CUTE! I was extremely skeptical about purchasing this dress! When I tried it on I was pleasantly surprised! I'm 5'7 and 155 pounds and the medium fit me like a glove! Stunning gown, but check the seams! I was invited to a black tie masquerade gala with only three days to find a gown! Thank you Amazon 2-day prime delivery for saving the day! This gown was absolutely incredible, made me feel sexy and stunning, and I got compliments all night! I’m 5‘2“ and 190 lbs, and I ordered the black gown in an XL. Because of my height, the front of the dress was perfectly floor-length, and the fishtail on the back actually became an elegant train. I did wear Spanx for extra support. The only issue I had was that I put the gown on right before leaving the house (which is the pic I’ve posted) shortly after getting to the event I started realizing that the dress started shifting/twisting on my body and the train was now off to the left and my left heel kept getting stuck on the lace. I think it was likely sewn incorrectly. Because of that, I’m actually going to return the dress because it’s defective, but I’m absolutely going to buy the same dress again! It’s a stunner and no one could believe it was a $50 dress from Amazon!

A perfect last minute dress I was invited to a black-tie banquet and had ordered a different dress for the occasion from another site. However, the package was delayed by weeks, and since I knew I could depend on the speed of Amazon Prime delivery, I decided to look for a backup dress on Amazon and came across this one. For reference, I'm 5'3", 118lbs, bust 34 inches, waist 26 inches, hip 37 inches. The tube top that comes with the dress was a bit loose on me, so I gave it to my friend. Thankfully I didn't need it anyway for how I planned to wear the dress. The dress was perfect and I got many compliments on it. Wearing it with the waist band right under my bust, it was the perfect length for me. The material also looks and feels high quality and sturdy. It was also thick enough that I didn't need to wear a slip or nipple pasties (I can only speak for the colour I ordered though). Out of the package it had a few light wrinkles, but really nothing that needs to be ironed out unless you're exceptionally particular. I received the package, hung it up overnight, and wore it out the following night without having to iron or steam it. I would definitely recommend this and I would buy it in another colour as well!

I’m not under any illusion that there is a perfect dress out there that will hide the fact that I just had a baby and am nowhere near my preferred size/shape. I just wanted something comfortable and not too frumpy. This dress made me feel beautiful. It flowed nicely over my body, wasn’t tight or restrictive, showed off my hard-earned breastfeeding cleavage without scaring anyone, and when I went to the bathroom to pump (left baby at home for the first time and was dreading this!!), there was a little clasp that easily unhooked for full access. As others have mentioned, the edges aren’t perfectly finished and it comes a little wrinkly, but for $40 it did the job of making me feel pretty during my first night out post-baby. I’m 5’6, around 185 pounds, and the large felt perfect. Should have probably also tried the medium to see if it fit better.
This dress was so cute I was looking for a long affordable open back dress and this was the perfect options, for reference im 5’9 so for my tall girlies it does reach the floor!

Can't tell its from Amazon! I was looking for a midi dress with elbow length sleeves, as I had a brand new tattoo to hide and a wedding to attend (I didn't want everyone to see my icky upper arm as it was healing). After multiple stores at two malls, I gave up and came to Amazon. Glad I did, because I found exactly what I was looking for. For reference, I am 5'1 at 160lbs with a 34" waist: a Medium was perfect for me, still room to fit a large due to the stretchy waist and roomy chest. I wore 3.5 inch heels, the front was 3 inches past my knees and the back was about 3 inches above my ankles. Definitely need to iron it as it wrinkles easy, but still good material. Over all, it looked quite elegant and was very comfy to wear for an extended time.

Pretty good for what it is I was low key hoping this dress might work for my upcoming (and very casual) wedding. But while it's a nice enough dress, it just doesn't feel like something you'd get married in. The dress itself is quite pretty and would make a nice bridesmaid or wedding guest dress. The material is soft and drapey, and is a nice mid weight. Has a bit of a choir robe vibe. Doesn't have a ton of stretch, but there is a discreet side zipper. I'm 5'2", 120lbs, 32B and a size small was about a size and a half too big for me, at least up top - it was loose in the bust, and the waist fit too low. The length was slightly too long when I wore 3" heels. The white color is a true white but looks somewhat softer in person than it does online. I wore nude undergarments and they weren't visible, though due to the soft, drapey nature of the fabric, I'd probably recommend wearing shapewear underneath anyway. Just for kicks, I tried this dress with a tulle crinoline overskirt both on top (which looked weird) and underneath (which added 30 visual pounds of weird lumps), so I wouldn't recommend going that route. All in all, this isn't a bad dress. It's pretty well made and has an elegant silhouette (on the right person, anyway). Just isn't something I'd want to get married in.

Flowy, ultra feminine and flattering I was not disappointed with this dress! The print is simply dreamy, the style is feminine and flowy, and the fit is flattering. According to their size chart, I should have ordered a medium for the waist size (I have a 30" waist) but I ordered a small because the rest of my measurements fell into the small range and the waist is elastic. I'm glad I ordered the small, a medium would have been frumpy and overwhelming on my frame. The color scheme, I ordered Pink Watercolour, was not as pictured. It's more of an orange palette, so I am disappointed in that somewhat. It's the only reason I removed a star. Can't wait to wear it in spring and early summer! I missed the opportunity to wear during the early, warmer days of fall. It's definitely too lightweight to be a fall dress where I live. I think the price is right on this, it has a luxurious, high quality look and feel to it.

Good quality and fits true to size I was pleasantly surprised how fast this arrived. Free Prime shipping. Key points that prompted me to choose this one over others: 1) Price - reasonable ($49.99 + free shipping) 2) Color matched - burgundy 3) Measurements were included and the hollow to floor is shorter than most - 57in. This was perfect bc I'm 5'6" and didn't need to hem the dress. I was planning to wear mid-heels. I ordered a size 6 bc I everything was close to my measurements except for the waist. Lol I took a chance and I'm glad I did bc it's empire waist, so I was able to zip it up. Negatives: 1) Downside to open back is that you need a good backless bra. Unfortunately, I can't wear sticky bras if I'm breastfeeding. It'll be awkwardly sticky. Maybe I'll try inserts. 2) The sleeves aren't as full as the picture. Wish they were a little fuller. Overall, I think this is a winner for a bridesmaid dress on a budget. The chiffon is passable. It's very light and the dress is lined. Just need to be careful on carpet bc it's a lint magnet. I think once steamed and at the venue, it'll look great in pictures.

This dress slayed in every way imaginable!! I was skeptical about ordering a size small for whatever reason. After reading the reviews about the amount of stretch that the dress has, I decided to go with my right mind and get a small. This dress did not disappoint...At all!! It fit me perfectly!! I am a 34B, 5'0 tall and I'm in-between a small/medium (6/8) in dress sizes. The dress does have a nice amount of stretch to it!! I want to order another one in red!!

Expect the Unexpected — Fits Surprisingly Well I was skeptical about the one sized fits all deal, but it works very well. It’s basically a maxi skirt with two pieces of fabric in front that wrap around the chest/upper body however you want it to, which gives it the perfect fit in the top. I wear a 36B in the chest, which meant I could go bra-less but they also included a bandeau so you could wear that with or without a bra if you needed to. I really liked that you could change up the dress however you wanted it to be. I went for a Grecian style. There aren’t really instructions so you have to be creative in styling it. There’s a video at the end of the photos on Amazon that show some of the different styles, but it won’t show every possible thing that can be done with it. The material was very soft and light, but slightly see through. Undergarments can be noticed if someone is looking for them. I had to use athletic tape on the nips so if you’re concerned about that, maybe get a backless bra, the chicken cutlets, or use the bandeau. The dress was long on me, but was fine (not touching the floor) with high heels. I’ve included a pic of the shoes to show how high they had to be. I would guess they’re about 4-6 inches. Not 100% certain. I’m 5’3.5” and weigh about 145lbs. Without heels the dress would’ve dragged on the floor. The fit in the waist was very good. If concerned about length, some reviews mention that this material is easy enough to cut at the end and it wouldn’t ruin the dress, but I wouldn’t risk it unless you’re good at sewing. There are two seams on the inside of the dress that I’d be concerned about cutting because it could unravel and potentially ruin it. If you’re shorter like me/don’t like wearing heels, and want to go that route but you’re not comfortable with sewing, get it altered professionally. You really can’t beat a dress like this for the price of $50. I’ve purchased $200 dresses before that didn’t quite match up to this one in terms of versatility, comfort, and your own personal style. Highly recommend.

Perfect dress for a formal wedding I was skeptical about this dress. I had a black tie wedding to attend, but didn’t want to spend an arm and a leg on a long, formal dress. Found this on Amazon and decided to give it a shot. I ordered one size up from my normal dress size and it fit perfectly. I wore spanks with it to smooth things out, and I got a ton of compliments. It is good quality for the price. It was very wrinkled when it arrived, but a good steaming took it right out. It also held up well for drinks, dinner and dancing. Lots of sitting and standing, and it still looked good at the end of the night. I would 100% recommend this dress to anyone who needs an affordable formal dress. My only complaint was that the lining was short. It came to my ankles. I wish it hand been closer to the floor; however, it probably ended up being a good thing for dancing. I am 5’6” for reference. *The photo makes the dress look bluer than it actually is. I consider it a true navy.

Great “formal” maternity option! I was slightly concerned about the reviews that said this dress was very thin (hello pregnancy lumps) but was pleasantly surprised! I didn’t try any fancy way of styling the dress, and wanted my arms to be covered so this was perfect for what I wanted. I would love to try additional styles when I’m not in such a time crunch. I did opt for a maternity shape wear so I didn’t have any underwear lumps, and I’m so glad I did. I am 5’4, about 175 and 30 weeks pregnant (size 6-8 pre-pregnancy) and there was plenty of room in this dress if I wanted to wear again. I will say it is kind of long and I could’ve paired short pumps to make it the perfect length but 7 1/2 months into pregnancy I just wasn’t feeling heels ;) I got so many compliments and people couldn’t believe it was a 2 day Prime purchase that wasn’t made for me. Would definitely buy again in other colors for different events!

Definitely a MIDI dress not mini dress I was so excited to get this dress as the reviews seemed decent and wrap styles tend to be flattering for most shapes and sizes. The color was beautiful, the fabric was very soft and flowing BUT it was certainly not the mini dress pictured or described. My daughter is in the image, you can see the dress comes to her lower calf area. If you are looking for a longer dress, it would be great. We were not. For reference my daughter is 5'1" and about 135 lbs. I am 5'2" and 128 but carry my weight in my middle more than her, this dress is a medium and fit us both (a little snugger on me through the waist/middle). Also, the color is "dark blue" and does not seem to be available any longer when I messaged customer service to try to get the mini dress. The color is very pretty though. Unfortunately for us, its going back as we do not have a need for a long dress right now.

True Review for Short Women I was so excited when the dress arrived and quickly rushed to try it on. Now, I'm a bit disappointed when I tried on the dress. I was planning to use this dress for the rehearsal dinner which is barely a month away. I really wish I felt more confident with this dress on but It fees like nothing looked flattering on me. Everything with a line can be seen through, you can see my bra line in the back, you can see directly where my belly ring is and my underwear color is almost noticeable. I ordered a large which typically fits me fine (M top, 8-10 jeans). I really thought this dress would have more support on the bra line and tummy area. Even if I attempted to wear a waist trainer, the material is so thin you would be able to see it. For being 5'2" I would have to cut off alot of fabric to match my height. As pretty it was in the photos, I am not planning to keep this dress.

Perfect Elopement Dress I was so happy with this dress! I have gained a few pounds over the last couple of years and was feeling pretty overwhelmed by the prospect of fitting into a wedding dress and actually being happy with how i look. After reading reviews I took a chance on this one. Husband and I eloped in Maui just the two of us. I didn’t want to spend a ton since our trip was so expensive and needed something that wouldn’t wrinkle in the suitcase. This dress fit the bill! I did wear a shaper body suit to smooth out some lumps and bumps. This dress is stretchy and hugged every curve! I love how sexy yet modest it is. I’ve received so many compliments! Everyone is blown away by my $50 Amazon wedding gown! For reference, I am 5’3”, 215 lbs, hourglass figure, 40 DDD. I ordered xxl and it was perfect. I probably could have sized down to xl. Able to slip dress over my head without using zipper. Dress did drag the ground as I wore flat sandals. I had to hold it up so it wouldn’t drag in the parking lot but I didn’t mind. It’s really beautiful and I felt really beautiful in it.

I am wearing this for my Covid affected wedding and cannot be happier... and I will be buying more in different colors!!!
