Shockingly Fabulous! I was so surprised how beautiful, simple and perfect this dress is. During these covid times, I decided to order a less expensive dress in case the small little wedding was cancelled. For $60 you cannot go wrong! I got so many compliments. Low down: I am 5'9 and 140 lbs. I purchased a size Small, which was perfect. I was still able to wear 2 inch heals. It was wrinkly upon arrival; as it was stuffed in a mailing envelope. You can use the steam function on your iron to gently flatten out the wrinkles. The fabric is sturdy enough and the lace sleeves were surprisingly comfortable - not itchy. It is tailored so nicely that I didn't have to mess with it. I did wear a thickly padded push-up bra to fill out the front - but that was a preference thing. A simple gold necklace, bracelet, earrings and face mask did the trick. I wore my hair up in a ponytail to accentuate the pretty neck line of the dress. I could not have gotten a better dress for this winter Christmas wedding.

Beautiful dress! I was so thrilled when I tried on this dress as it fit perfectly! I am 165 cm (5’4”) and usually wear size 4 clothes. I am usually hesitant when it comes to buying clothes online- especially an elegant dress for a ball - but I was in a time crunch. I ordered a few other dresses also as a backup, same size and none of them fit but this dress in size 4 was perfect! I received a lot of compliments and I felt very comfortable wearing it. I started the night in high heel pumps and changed into sandals that had only a one inch heel and the length was perfect for both.

Lalalalove this dress! I was so tired at this point of the day but this dress was everything such a lovely color great fit (I’m 5’1 163lbs 33 weeks prego. Got a size large) perfect flow to the floor didn’t trip over my feet so many compliments and love the fact that you can wear it multiple ways, comes with a lil strapless bra cover slip. A little see through but nothing a slip won’t fix.

Cute I was SOO worried this dress wouldn’t fit me, I ordered three other options just in case. If I would have been a pound heavier, it wouldn’t have lol but it worked out for our court wedding. (Here I am: 8 1/2 months pregnant, weighing 137 pounds and I’m 5”2) I got a medium. The dress is stretchy but very tight fitting at the same time (which is what I wanted) . Would love to own this dress when I’m no longer pregnant in diff colors! <3 :)

Fits well! I was struggling to find a big bust friendly dress days before a wedding and this was perfect. The material is thick, not see through. Perfect for slightly cold weather or slightly warm. Worth it! I was surprised by how much I liked this amazon dress! For reference I’m 5”3 and definitely could have gotten it hemmed by a couple of inches but I bought this dress last minute and didn’t have time.m. Surprisingly Good Quality, Fits Well I was surprised by the quality of this dress. I have a glum outlook usually when it comes to buying things from big chain websites; it seems like all the clothes look like China-made cheap clothes. This was a pleasant surprise; it could have been bought at any store. Don't get me wrong, it is no bridal store dress, but it is more comfortable. I love the lace top and that the back dips lower than the front. With a large front, a lot of these shows too much cleavage; this one does not. I love that. The A-line cut is flattering like always, and I love that at 5'4, it does not drag on the floor. It comes right to the top of my feet, so if you are taller, it may be a weird length on the calf or ankle. If you are shorter it may drag. I will say the only thing I did not like was the sleeves, they were a bit open for me, and you could see the side boob and armpit since it is only a small cap sleeve. I will still wear it but probably with a sweater, its a winter even anyway.

No stretch. I was surprised to see that this is double lined, and the construction is super solid. Although, I was expecting more of a neoprene like fabric given the way it held its shape in the photos. For reference, I'm 5 ft tall, and fluctuate between 118-123 regularly, with AA bitties. Normally I wear a small in most clothing, and run in a US 4-6, so I got a small based on the size chart. It's super cute on but as you can see, I'm not so blessed in the chest, and the shoulders are larger than mine to fill it in. There is little flexibility, and stretch in the stomach area and I'm seriously considering wearing this with some shape wear since my stomach area is a little fluffier these days than I'd like. Either way, if you're between sizes like me, size up and expect to get it tailored. Thankfully I have a sewing machine and I'm capable to fix up the shoulders a bit so there's not so much of a gap, but I'm not so crazy about the stiff fabric. I love this dress more in the pictures, it's' cute on me, but not amazing.

Comfortable and fits perfectly I was surprised with a last-minute invitation to a schmancy event, and found this pretty dress was thankfully available for next-day delivery. I liked how it looked on those who wore it close-fitting on top, so I ordered with that in mind; knowing it was stretchy, I ordered the size slightly down from my bust measurement but that still fit my waist - that's important, because the satin sash that sits at high waist/underbust does not give at all, so is the only part that MUST fit. Everything else is either stretchy or flowy. I'm 5'7" and the length was perfect - just kissing the floor with short heels on (I think 1-1/2" or 2"). Honestly felt like wearing jammies, but more sparkly. I did see some product photos that made the top appear slightly sheer, but that was not my experience - I wore a regular nude (for my skin tone) bra and had no show-through. I haven't tried to wash it yet, but it feels so well-constructed that I'm not worried. Very pleased with my choice.
Far too big I was swimming in this dress! For reference, I’m 5’3” petite/narrow-framed and around 100-105lbs. Petite gals, beware!

I love how I was able to control the split height with the zipper. I definitely stood out from the bridesmaids and complimented the bride. I will definitely wear this again. I'm 5'5" around 140lbs 34D/36C and the Small fits perfectly.

Such a beautiful gown! I was the mom of the groom and got so many compliments on it! It was so comfy and flowy, never hot of uncomfortable. The color was my favorite and the bride was really happy with my choice (She just told me get something with sparkle)! I was truly thrilled with this purchase and the designer! Also, the length was perfect for me at 5’8” and 2 3/4” heels and didn’t require alterations’

It's a YES for petite women! I was the officiant at my brother's wedding, which meant I had to wear either black or muted tones. It was a rooftop terrace wedding in S Florida so keep that in mind. I'm considered petite based on height (5'1") and weight (102-105lbs) but have a weird athletic build so I have difficulty finding dresses that fit my thicker arms. I'm not joking; I must have ordered 15 outfits to try on before the wedding ... nothing fit. I looked like a kid playing dress up. Ugh! I'm a mom of 6 ... so I was shocked nothing fit properly at my stage in the game. So I ordered this dress with low expectations, but loved how the women looked in the review photos. I ordered 4P, which I assume is size 4 petite (I normally wear junior sizes) and I ordered 3" gold heels to give me some extra height and not shorten my legs even more by wearing black shoes. (I don't love the style of shoes, but anyway.) When I tried on the dress I loved the top fit but it felt like too much material everywhere else and much longer than expected. My SO said I actually looked frumpy and of course that made me feel awful! Once I arrived at wedding venue, the wedding coordinator said I was dressed absolutely perfectly ... classy for the occasion. She probably tells everyone that! haha. But ... I got many complements on this dress from guests I've never met before. So that's my review. Hope it helps you! I included pics: full length/front, neckline detail, neckline/back view, and me with bride and groom after the wedding ceremony.

Almost perfect!! I was told by my colleagues that I had the best dress that night. Super elegant and sexy but not trashy. I do have to make alterations, first I stitched about 2 inches in the chests area as it is very low cut. Then I cut about 3-4 inches in the bottom since I am only 5’1. I wore the altered length with 4 inches heels and long fitted skirt to keep me warm. FYI, this is a very thin dress and below the knee is mesh material no lining so it is see through. Fit wise, it is snug which I like. For 5’1 112lbs, I have plenty of curves so finding a perfect fitted dress is challenging. For reference my body measurement is 34-24-35. If you don’t mind super low cut cleavage and is 5’6 or taller this dress is for you. This is a true XS, without the alterations I made I would have given this 10stars.

I actually ended up buying this in two colors, the champagne in size small and the white in size medium. Great for pregnancy too! I was trying to find a dress to wear to my sister's wedding while 5 months pregnant. This was a great option. I'm 5'3" and 117lbs with A baby belly and this dress was a perfect fit. Maternity dresses i boughr on Amazon and tried on were made of Jersey net fabrics were a little cheap looking, but this had a nice flow to it. It is a little see through, so be sure you have some type of under garment.

WORTH THE PURCHASE I was undecided in the size but I went with a medium and it fit perfectly. The color was PERFECT and material was easy to iron. However it is very long so if your wearing heels be careful I wore flats and if drug the ground but I’m not upset about it I’m 5’5. It was light and flowy. You can’t really adjust the neck part if you’re pregnant it gives a pull because of the belly.

Gorgeous dress!!! I was very nervous about ordering a dress online, but this dress was surprisingly well made! It did not look cheap and the fabric felt nice to the touch. It has a corset back which made adjusting for a snug fit on top super easy. The straps are pretty long, but that makes for easy alterations as needed or you could just pin them down to your liking in the back. The bustline cut does go pretty deep for me (i'm pretty busty) so there was definitely a good amount of cleavage going on. This dress is LONG. I am 5'6 about 215lbs and ordered a size 16. In the photo I had on about a 4 inch heel and the dress still dragged the ground a bit. So, if you're shorter definitely plan to have the length altered. The company e-mailed offering to make the dress to my custom measurements, but I just went with the standard measurements instead and it fit wonderfully. Seriously, BUY THIS! It is so flattering!

Turned out beautiful for my special day! I wasn't sure what to expect, when ordering this dress for my wedding... I purchased my normal size (XXL) and it fit perfectly! The lace and liner underneath have a little bit of give and are not super restrictive. I received SO many compliments on this wedding dress, and even had a family member ask me to save it for her! I am 5'8", and the very back of the lace on the skirt did drag on the ground, so it got dirty. Something to keep in mind, and I made sure to hide it during our photos. The only negative I will say, is that the seamstresses put a black dot on the white fabric, as a guide to sew the liner, underneath the lace. With the way the dress fit on me, you could see the black dot under the lace... and it was on one of my boobs, so NOT ideal. Luckily, I had painted my toenails white, so I carefully moved the lace aside and painted a spot of white polish over the black dot, and that did the trick! All in all, I was shocked to receive so many compliments on a wedding dress that only cost me $40! It was comfortable, and I felt beautiful in it... Highly recommend.

I went back and forth looking at reviews and pictures of this dress for over a month before I finally decided to give it a try and I’m so glad I did! The first one I ordered the lace on top was sewn on uneven but they shipped me another one within 2 days and the second one was so much better. It’s the perfect amount of sexy and classy. I’m 5’1, 210lbs and curvy. This dress fit like a glove, seriously. I only slipped it over my head, I never tried the zipper due to other peoples reviews. The lace is beautiful and stretchy. The length for me was perfect. I wore 3inch heels. The train of the dress did drag on the ground and get super dirty but I only used this dress to elope in and won’t need to wear it again. If I have a formal event to go to I would definitely order this dress in black. If you are looking for a beautiful dress on a budget I suggest trying this dress and if it doesn’t fit well the returns are easy!

Nice work/dressy pants! I went by the measurements and the fit is great. Maybe just a titch long (I'm 5'6", waist 35, and got XL) but there is enough room in the rise that I can wear them high at my natural waist and they don't drag on the ground. They have belt loops, nice deep pockets, and a flat hook and zip closure on the fly. There's elastic on the back of the waistband. The material is thin suiting, kind of a drapy polyester twill, it has a flat, cool, smooth surface. It drapes great, doesn't seem to wrinkle, and since it is all-polyester, it does need a good spray of Static Guard to keep it from clinging. They flow when walking and are comfortable to wear, though the surface is kind of cool and slick. The pant legs are the same width all the way down, with two pleats in the front gathering the excess fullness to the waist. The back has no pleats and is thus less voluminous, so it shows the curve of the butt a bit more. I like the fullness of these and the material won't wrinkle easily, so they'd be nice for travel.

Really beautiful color and design I went for the light blue, which, btw, is exactly that and not teal! There is no shade of green in this, so the dress looks a bit different, color-wise than what is displayed online, but still very, very pretty. The fabric is the scuba type (Chloroprene rubber), so it is very thick and does have a rubbery feel. This is to give you the compression that bandage dresses provide, which it certainly accomplishes. I am 5'5, 130 lbs, I got an M. I could have went for an S, but didn't want to feel like I can't breath in it. Turns out I made the right decision, as the M fits like a glove, with plenty of compression. I think it's an excellent dress for special occasions. The reasons I'm taking off a star are: 1) the feel of the fabric and 2) some loose threads at the bottom of the zipper, where it gasps a bit. So I need to get a needle and some thread and secure that area. No biggie though, still love this!

Size down! I wish I could love it! I ordered a small, I normally wear a 4/6 in jeans and an 8 in dresses. 5’2, 115lbs, 36 bust, 28 waist, 37.5 hips. I’m returning since I don’t have time before the event I need it for to make an exchange, but I would buy this again in a smaller size for another occasion.

Lovely and Affordable I wore this as my Reception Dress because my expensive Wedding Dress had a long train and we didn't want people to step on it. The quality of this dress is incredible. The bodice is embellished with beads and the embellishment gives the dress a glamorous sparkle. If you're a tall girl you're in luck. I'm 5'1 so very petite and the dress was really long, my grandmother had to remove over a foot of material from the bottom, cut the tulle and hem the lining. I usually wear a size 12/14 in street clothes and ended up fitting perfectly in a size 18 (Remember lady's wedding dress sizes are different from street clothes.) There was a little spare room in the waist and in the bust but the lace-up back kept everything in place. This dress is super light compared to my other gown and I was able to dance comfortably and move as needed. I was wearing a hoop skirt under it and that's what gives it that extra body. All in all, I think this was a great dress and I feel like I got a good deal on it.

Perfect Engagement pictures dress! I wore this dress for engagement pictures and they turned out excellent! Its a beautiful dress and well worth the money. It's made very well too! I am 5'6 225ish lbs for anyone trying to decide which size to order. I ordered a size 18 just in case it ran small. I'm normally in a 16/18 and and it fit well on me since I wanted a slight bit of room, versus being overly snug. I did have a slight bit of extra wiggle room in my chest area so you could possibly size down if you have a smaller chest. For someone shorter than I am it may be pretty long to wear with flip flops, etc but it can also be hemmed. For reference I am a DD. It mostly depends how you prefer dresses to fit. For my height the dress was definitely long enough especially if you wanted to wear heels with it. I was barefoot so it was slightly long on me but not to where I was tripping on it. The length would definitely be perfect with heels too. I would absolutely buy this dress again since it was a win in my book. If you're considering buying this dress I highely recommend it!

Loved it!!! I wore this dress to my daughter's wedding and I absolutely loved it! The fit was amazing. I normally have a very hard time finding dresses that fit me well and don't make me look pregnant with my mom-stomach. The boob area had the perfect amount of stretch and cleavage. Highly recommend!

Beautiful Dress/ Terrible Packaging I wore this dress to my niece wedding. I'm 5'4 and 216 pounds. I orederd the 2x and thank goodness I did. If you’re 4’11” make sure you use heels I wore this dress to the Bridgerton event, and for it to work for me without having to hem it, I had to wear it with the waist of the dress tied at my underbust, so kinda empire style. It will be fine once fall and winter weather hit here in CA.

Highly recommend this dress and highly recommend purchasing the bra that I got if you’re needing one!

Perfect! I wore this for my daughter’s wedding. I wasn’t expecting it to fit so good. The fit was nice, and the color was perfect for a fall wedding. The material is not the really soft type but for the price it worked great. For reference I am 5’3 and wear a medium in tops.

Arrived Fast and Fit Great I wore this for my elopement and ordered it 4 days before the big day. I was so worried it was going to be cheap looking or not fit, it looked and fit great. I ordered a medium and it was true to size. I am 130lbs and 5'7" and the length was perfect in heels. I did have to use a steam iron to take out more than a few stubborn wrinkles. The fabric was not see through but I was not able to wear a regular bra because it had plunging neckline and was backless. I wore a stick on bra, no wardrobe malfunctions on the big day. I am not large chested but there was room for B cups. The jumper was a lovely cream color, maybe winter white? Very flowy, casual. The mid-section has a panel lining so you can rest assured no panty lines show, just wear a neutral color underwear.

Great fix! Not see through I wore this for my engagement photos and it was perfect! I wore a slip underneath just to be safe and also did a gentle wash and no heat tumble dry to get any wrinkles out. The sleeves are super cute and the dress was very comfortable. I ordered a large, I’m 5’5” and 150lbs!

Nice dress for the price!! I wore this for my pandemic wedding at home. It fit well (though it gaped a bit at the back neckline, near zipper, my hair covered it). I finished it off with a petticoat, and my husband helped me iron out the wrinkles. I'm 5'4", and my measurements are 41, 33, 40, (wear size 12 pants, and either 36DD or 34DDD depending on the bra ). I bought a large (I wanted a tight fit, and there was some stretch in the fabric, but not much), and I wore some shapewear to smooth out my back rolls. Be aware, this dress is nice for the price, and I felt quite pretty, but the white will show the color of your underwear. I strongly recommend a petticoat and skin-colored undergarments if you're getting the white dress.

Beautiful But Some Things To Know I wore this for our 10 year wedding anniversary and it was beautiful! I got lots of compliments on the dress. I don’t normally wear things like this, but I felt fairly comfortable in it most of the night. I was able to go braless. I think it did a pretty good job of hiding my mommy tummy (after two big boys, I have ab separation) without the use of spanx. A couple of issues, I found several unsewn seems or areas where the seams weren’t sewn properly. Fortunately they were all in the bottom layer and they were unnoticeable unless you searched for them. The other issue was that it was very tight at the bottom and hard to walk in at times, but that could be subjective. My measurements: Chest: 37” (I’m a small B) Waist: 33” Hips: 46.5”

Great fabric and "glitter" didn't fall off I wore this to a wedding this past weekend. I'm 5'3", currently 190-200lbs, a busty 38DD and purchased this in size 14. It fit great with a "foundation garment" underneath to smooth out my "rolls". It was snug, but not tight or uncomfortable. Ordering a dress sight unseen, without trying it on, I was worried it would take a few attempts to get the right size, or that I would get stuck with a dress that was baggy and ill fitting, but I was pleasantly surprised that it was perfect on the first try. I'm short, so the dress really needed to be hemmed, but I didn't bother with it and just wore thick-heeled platform high heels and occasionally lifted the skirt when I needed to walk faster (less carefully). One thing to remember is that the back is lower cut as well so any foundation garment you wear has to be lower cut too - the first one I tried was too high in the back and visible, so I had to try a different style from the same brand. I'm sorry the pictures aren't very good, but the only pictures I have that I'm in so far are ones taken with my fiancee's phone, and he and his son (who took one of the pictures) are terrible at taking pictures. I would highly recommend and will order from Ever-Pretty again.

Mother of the Bride dress I wore this to my daughter’s wedding and received so many compliments. I am 5’4” and weigh 165, normally wear an 8-10 and I am wearing a large. I could have gone with medium but wanted to give myself a little extra room. I love the off the shoulder look but you can also adjust the top to wear it different ways. The material is thick so it looks quality, not cheap. And it has stretch so it’s comfortable and not stiff. As Described Special Occasion Dress I'd like to say I'm totally in love here, but I'm not. However: I am absolutely in "like" - and this dress and jacket are very good for your average special occasion. The dress IS a body con deal, and the side ruched areas actually help if you have a bit of a tummy. My taste is that it's not quite mom of the bride/groom quality (and my son just engaged, so I'm thinking in that direction...yeah!) but still great for attending a wedding. At first blush, I thought it might be just a tiny bit too tight, but once I settled into it, I realized that it fit perfectly. The jacket floats more than closes, which you need to notice in the ad - it doesn't button/have buttons. I am terrible at getting selfies in dresses, but will ask my husband to get a photo. I like this and it's a great dress to have on hand, as we all have invites, etc. spring up. I hope to use the dress itself with other jackets, too. It's a good "shell" to pair like that, IMO. Finally: The size chart is accurate, which is valuable information. At 5'8" I measure right at the 20W stats on the chart, with my hips lower, and the dress fits pretty perfectly - I am 48 - 40 - 48. For a fairly versatile dress set, the price seems fine, and they have it in a good selection of colors for formal wear. I recommend it. :)

Comfortable & stretchy I'd say the only thing I disliked about this dress was the boning. It was uncomfortable and jabbed under my chest (36D), the reasoning for 4 stars. I cut tiny slits and took all of them out and it made the dress perfect for a wedding I attended. I had to hem the bottom because I'm shorter but overall, loved it. The stretchiness, the ribbing helps conceal your stomach and accentuates your body.

Nice formal for the price. I’m 5’ 6”, 165 lbs, 38D - I bought the XL and it fits perfectly. Usually I’m a Large dress but due to other reviews and the size chart, I got the XL and I’m glad I did. The Large would have fit buttonwood have been tighter and I would have been self-conscious all night long. Regarding the length, I’m 5’ 6” and wore cowboy boots and the dress brushed the ground. I ordered the brown and as you can see from the pictures, it’s more copper and changes color in different lighting. The fabric is easily snagged, specifically with rough dry winter hands. I wore a regular bra and had to keep the straps on the edges of my shoulders to hide them. The back does come up high enough to hide the back of a bra. I wore shapewear bike shorts which worked well but did not line up high enough to match the location of the seam under the chest. But it was fine. It’s a great dress for being inexpensive. I’d buy it again. Fabric has stretch and is comfortable.

used as my wedding dress! I'm 5'0 145lbs and I originally ordered a large- I ended up returning it and getting a medium which fit PERFECT! I had a small country rustic wedding in my front yard and reception in our pole barn. My husband wore jeans, a white shirt and cowboy boots- this worked well to show off my boots too :) I didn't want an actually wedding gown so this was PERFECT! I got so many compliments on my dress and how it was just perfect! I like the fact I could take this on vacation and dress it up with some color. I did add a sash with a flower to it which turned out nicely. The dress is well made and has a liner so i didn't have to wear a slip. It's stretchy so the sizing is generous. This was super comfortable and I feel the neckline was very slimming flattering and classy. It was also nice because I didn't have to figure out a necklace to wear :)

I like it!! I’m 5’0, 170 lbs., and have a larger chest (38DDD). I was unsure of this dress because of my larger chest, but I think it actually minimizes it a little!! I read a bunch of these reviews, so I was skeptical—especially the ones that claimed the V-neck area came up too high, and didn’t work for some. On me, the neck hangs just right, doesn’t ride up, or too high. I’ve only just tried it on, so I’m unsure of what it’ll do while wearing it out! I’ll update if there’s any issues!! I don’t like form-fitting clothes, and I usually try to hide my stomach, but because of the bunching mid-section, it hides it better than I thought! The bottom is more “pencil-skirt” shaped, which I don’t normally like, but I think it works ok. I’m planning on wearing to a summer wedding, and the fabric is heavier than I’d like, especially in this 100°+ heat right now!! But during Fall/Winter/Spring, it should be perfect! P.S. I didn’t know what to do with the waist tie, brought it around the front, when I guess it’s supposed to go around the back and tie on the side lol! Oops! So just ignore that part in the photo!! 🤣 Anyway, I’d recommend it…just know it’s a little heavier material, and form-fitting, and I honestly think the V-neck is fine!! Oh, the other thing people complained about were the arms—yeah, if arms raised, they’re a little odd, but you don’t normally walk around with your arms raised up in the air, so I’m not worried!! Will update if there’s any issues after wearing it out!!

True to size, ruching is a bit much, top does fold down, beautiful color I'm 5'0" and about 112 lbs and ordered a size small in the blue. The blue is a beautiful color, and the dress is soft and stretchy. On some dresses, the ruching sort of camouflages my belly. I didn't feel that this one did and wore some seamless, smoothing underwear with it. I wore a bra with clear straps since the top doesn't cover bra straps. The top part does keep coming undone. It would be much better if it were stitched in place. I also wish the dress had a bit more structure--I kept adjusting the ruching. I did find that it rode up a bit as I walked around, but not too bad. It was pretty short on me, and more flattering than some considering how short I am. Overall I'd say this is probably better on someone a bit taller and thinner than me. A decent buy, as long as you don't mind the top coming unfolded.

Surprisingly decent quality dress I’m 5’1, 109lbs, 32-34A, basically flat chested. I haven’t had much luck with buying clothes from Amazon except for some leggings and tank tops for the gym. I read lots of reviews before buying anything! I was really quite pleasantly surprised at how flattering this dress was on me! Even husband approved and he’s very picky! Love the satin like feel of the material which makes it flowy and the slit above the knee is very sexy! Also, it’s a great plus that the straps are adjustable and the tie knot at the chest as well. I ordered XS and I feel it is true to size although there is a bit of a give since the back is smocked so, it doesn’t feel tight. It is perfect to wear to a party with eating involved because the waist is empire and the dress still gives a sexy look without sacrificing style to accommodate bloat in the belly. I have yet to see how this holds up after washing.

It arrived folded and the wrinkles wouldn’t steam out with my travel steamer. I had to wash and hang dry then steam. The pictures are after about 5 wear, wash, and hangs BEFORE I steam for work. As you can see, I could probably get away with just hanging dry too. I took pictures in natural light and with the room light on. It’s more of a bubble gum pink, not Barbie pink. I won’t iron and hate steaming so I was looking for a mostly polyester material and wanted to stay far away from cotton and the like. As a teacher I wear this with a cami underneath even though it’s not thin enough to need it, untucked and with dress pants. Works great. Update: 2mo later. The more you wear, wash, & hang dry, the less you have to steam or iron this shirt. I’m ordering the red next. Update: I’ve since purchased more colors: light blue, red (like a brick red), black, dark purple, and white. Love these shirts. Yeah, there might be a loose thread here or there but nothing a snip of the scissors can’t fix. The polyester silky material means the black won’t fade like other materials. The only reason I haven’t purchased the royal blue or green is because I have other button downs in those colors. I’ve only washed and worn the white twice and steamed it but after the third or forth wear, they don’t really even need steamed. I just hang dry on a hanger. To the seller: PLEASE make more colors! -a lilac, a coral/peach, and pinstripe options would be great!!

Budget Wedding Yay! I'm 5'3 and wore a size 4 in this dress. I weighed around 120-125 lbs at the time. I normally wear size 4 so this fit true to size for me. With heels, this dress flowed perfectly to the floor, not enough to warrant getting hemmed. But I did need to lift it slightly to walk comfortably. This dress is cheaper if you purchase it directly from EverPretty website. I paid about $34 for it in March 2020. Tips: - Material is not stretchy so make sure you order the exact size that will fit you - The little jewels on the top lace part can fall off, BUT they are arranged in a way that you can not tell the difference at all and it won't affect the integrity of the design - It comes with many wrinkles when you open the package. I was on a budget so I just ironed it myself. Iron worked fine to smooth it out and did not ruin the fabric. - If you get a light color, it's actually quite transparant, your underwear will show (at least the shape of it). I used a nude color underdress (Maidenform) to solve the problem. - The lacy part has a fine mesh covering (nude color) that prevents your skin from showing (in case you're worried about the upper part). - There's no built in bra, but if you're a C or above, you won't need one. If you're a B or smaller, like me, I recommend the underdress. It comes with a built in bra & it's strapless. Now the review. I'm a broke college student that's been in love with my hubby since high school and we finally got hitched after 8 years of dating. Even if I could only afford a small civil ceremony, I still wanted to wear a gorgeous white dress. I wanted something elegant and classy, and nothing too extravagant. After months of eyeing David's Bridal & Lulu's online, I decided to search Amazon thinking why not. And I was grateful that the dresses fit my budget better here, but still gorgeous. After another 1-2 months of narrowing down my choices on Amazon, I finally decided on this dress. I can't believe how good it looked for only $34!! I got loads of compliments.

Even if I could only afford a small civil ceremony, I still wanted to wear a gorgeous white dress. I wanted something elegant and classy, and nothing too extravagant. After months of eyeing David's Bridal & Lulu's online, I decided to search Amazon thinking why not. And I was grateful that the dresses fit my budget better here, but still gorgeous. After another 1-2 months of narrowing down my choices on Amazon, I finally decided on this dress. I can't believe how good it looked for only $34!! I got loads of compliments.

Budget Wedding Yay! I'm 5'3 and wore a size 4 in this dress. I weighed around 120-125 lbs at the time. I normally wear size 4 so this fit true to size for me. With heels, this dress flowed perfectly to the floor, not enough to warrant getting hemmed. But I did need to lift it slightly to walk comfortably. This dress is cheaper if you purchase it directly from EverPretty website. I paid about $34 for it in March 2020. Tips: - Material is not stretchy so make sure you order the exact size that will fit you - The little jewels on the top lace part can fall off, BUT they are arranged in a way that you can not tell the difference at all and it won't affect the integrity of the design - It comes with many wrinkles when you open the package. I was on a budget so I just ironed it myself. Iron worked fine to smooth it out and did not ruin the fabric. - If you get a light color, it's actually quite transparant, your underwear will show (at least the shape of it). I used a nude color underdress (Maidenform) to solve the problem. - The lacy part has a fine mesh covering (nude color) that prevents your skin from showing (in case you're worried about the upper part). - There's no built in bra, but if you're a C or above, you won't need one. If you're a B or smaller, like me, I recommend the underdress. It comes with a built in bra & it's strapless. Now the review. Would 100% purchase again!! I’m 5’3” 135lbs, Amazon suggested I order medium but after reviewing the comments I decided to go with a small, and I am so glad I did! The fabric is stretchy but still compresses but isn’t stretchy to the point of transparency. Even after chasing my two year old around all night this dress stayed put and I didn’t have to worry about bending over to pick him up. I myself do not have a rock hard body after having a baby I do have a mom pooch so I do recommend wearing a faja with this dress if you know what I’m talking about cause it will mold to your body and show every dimple nook crack and crevice. But other than that the bunched up fabric hid a lot of what my faja couldn’t control! The built in corset is comfortable and super supportive I didn’t have to wear a bra I thought I would be uncomfortable and poke me through out the night and it absolutely did not. The zipper does stick a but once you get to the middle where there’s a hem in the fabric but honestly I was able to just pull the dress right on without unzipping it. I would 10/10 recommend this dress!

Flattering cut, soft, well-made, comfortable, perfect fit! I'm 5'3", 127 lbs, 34B and the XS fits perfectly with enough stretch to accommodate tad smaller or larger frame than myself. Love it I'm 5'3", aprox 140lbs, 38-29-40, 32F bra. I purchased a size 12. It arrived in exactly a week. The dress was lovely. I would buy from this seller again. All of the girls at prom who spent $700 on their dresses could not believe I found this gem on Amazon for under $200. The only problems I had with this dress were entirely personal and forseeable because I am a picky short hourglass. I think that if you love this dress as much as I did and the only thing holding you back from buying it is the lack of reviews, you should throw caution to the wind and order it. The dress was about 4-5in too long, which I was expecting because I am considered petite. The dress had excess material bunch at the waist, which I again expected because of my chest to waist ratio. I did have the dress altered and it was a completely awful experience and waste of money, but it's okay because my dress was still very beautiful and no amount of crooked hemming could change that. The dress does not look like the advertised picture and I was completely okay with that because I feel that what I received was more unique. The slip is more gold than nude. There are four layers: two soft gold slips and 2 mesh layers. Up close the decaling is a bit old ladyish or vintage, whichever term you prefer. The neckline of the sheer decaling goes to my collar bones. The plunging neckline of the slip goes a little past where a normal bra band at the front is. I purchased a clear straped and backless bra from Amazon and taped my chest in. The back of the dress to the waist is sheer with decaling all over it. Sheer enough that you could see my bra band through it when I tried to wear a normal bra, but has enough rhinestones (?) on it that you couldnt see my horrendous tan lines. The sleeves are absolutely beautiful, but were bigger on me than I had liked. If you find a good seamstress, it shouldn't be a problem to fix them. The zipper in the back is not obnoxiously noticeable and goes down to the top of my bum where it gets stuck in the same place everytime I zip it up. Jimmy it a little and you'll be fine. The dress itself is timeless and I felt like the belt was a good idea, but did not go with the dress. I cut it off and wore a belt I purchased from Amazon. I think that this dress can be worn by any age. If you're worried about modesty, I'm sure you could find a tanktop to wear underneath. Or if you're more talented than me (and the seamstress I paid a leg and an arm for) you could probably sew something to be more conservative.

Stretchy, most likely nursing friendly. I’m 5’3”, weigh 158, 34D bra size and I am currently 40+ weeks pregnant. I purchased this for family pictures once the baby is out but tried it on anyway. I bought a small and it falls right above my feet at this point, will probably be longer after my stomach shrinks a bit. There is a lot stretch in this dress, which I’m glad I purchased the small considering my current measurements. I would also say this dress is very cleavage-friendly and it will probably be easy to nurse in once baby is here (which was another factor in purchasing this dress). The material is like a slinky Jersey, which gives it extra stretch. I hope this review helps. The only snafu I had was I purchased this item on Monday, it was received early Wednesday morning. The notification I received was it said it was delivered and handed directly to a resident when I wasn’t home at the time it was delivered. But it was safely in front of my door. Just thought the notification was weird.

my new best friend/ I got 4 I’m 5’5.5”, 163-ish, regular sizes 10-12 Large, age 55, w/ extra saggy upper arms cuz I shed 50-100 extra # I’ve carried my entire adult life. Goal: summer shirt, down to elbow (to cover bat wings), below spare tire. This fabric is breezy, girly, dress up-or-down, will drip dry super fast (= perfect for travel) and LOOKS AWESOME in all my worst areas while standing. Not tight (even with baggy arms: order your normal size). It looks great from all 3 sides: the front, the back, the side. Caveat: Looks least awesome sitting at table on Zoom screen. In real life, and when not at table, one would see pattern at hips/ belly. In the end, I ordered 4: 1 black/ beige flowery stripes, 1 white w/ flowers, and 2 blue flowers.

Could be good for the right person? I’m 5’5” and 105 lb with a small chest so the dress was too big on me and I had to return it. If they made an XS it probably would have worked. The details on the embroidery were super cute, but a lot of them had long, loose threads which I don’t think is acceptable for a nearly $50 dress. The other reviews were correct that the fabric is not hemmed and would likely come apart if someone looked at it too hard. I didn’t have much trouble with the zipper. Stunning! I'm over the moon with this dress! I'm 5'5", ~198 lbs, ~40" waist & chest, ~50" hips/gut, and ordered size 20 Plus. This is also the first dress I've worn in my adult life (part of that due to concern of finding a flattering dress at my size range). What drew me to it was the shape and overall aesthetic. The crossed neck is incredibly gorgeous and flattering, while the side bow is a nice finishing touch. The details on it draw the eyes away from my midsection in my opinion which makes me tickled pink, all puns intended. I love it, my partner loves it, and I'm thrilled with the order. The material is so soft and smooth and extremely pleasing in sensation against the skin to wear. The length is just perfect as well, nice and long without draping directly onto the floor. It's wonderfully made and feels of really nice quality. I'm going to do my best to take care of it and enjoy this one for a long time to come. Dresses might have to be back on my wardrobe menu, thanks to this Hanna Nikole gem.

Fits like a glove! Super comfortable! I'm 5'5", 143 lbs, athletic build and I bought a Medium. The dress fit perfectly. I normally don't like wrap around dresses or shirts but this is a faux wrap around. It's made to look like it's wrapping but it's sewn that way so it fits so much nicer without all the flaps and ties. It's very comfortable and the material has a nice thickness to it. Nice and stretchy but not so stretchy that you have much more room for expansion. It definitely is made true to size. I will say, if you are more smaller chested, you probably will have to pin the "v" neck area as it might start to gap a bit. You should give it a try. It's such a nice dress and so well made!

Absolutely stunning! I'm 5'5", DD gal, about 155lbs. I read in another review that the dress runs large, so I ordered a Medium and my gosh. This dress fit like a gem! I wore it as a wedding guest and I got SO MANY compliments. I felt so pretty in it! Pro: Let's talk about the bust. Like I said, I'm a DD gal. I did not need a bra for this. It was so supportive and made my boobs look fantastic! I was also able to dance all over the place without a worry of anything popping out. Con-ish: I will say the inside seams of the shoulders were a little itchy and tugged as a result of the weight of my upper half, but not to an uncomfortable or untolerable point. Con: I did not like the padding it came with. The pads were too small and thick. I ended up cutting them out and replacement them with larger pads. Pro: POCKETS The pockets on this dress are a HUGE Pro for me. They were quite sizeable! I was able to fit a small makeup bag, my phone, a cleaning cloth, and a comb on my person. Couldn't even tell I had anything in them at all and nothing fell out! Con-ish: I have a short torso. This dress has no boning in the waist, so it would bunch up on me. I'd have to pull the waist fabric down every so often, but this wasn't too big of a deal. Con-ish?: This ain't a dress you can put on by yourself. You WILL need someone to help lace you up and down. Final: No regrets with this dress at all. Absolutely adore it. Hope I get to wear it again soon!

Oh, the sleeves are smocked elastic also so you can adjust to your liking. Good for all kinds of occasions.

Perfect and not too revealing! I'm 5'9" and 205 lbs, usually wear a size 14. It fit perfectly! I needed something that was actually knee length and not too revealing at the neckline for a professional event. This dress was perfect! I paired it with beige closed toe heels (life stride) and my clutch also came from Amazon. It looked especially cute with rose gold jewelry. I was so happy with it and recieved plenty of compliments! It's rare to find a dress that's truly knee length when you're 5'9" and I was so pleased that this one was long enough. Be aware that it's a very dark navy blue. I do recommend a skin tone bra underneath because if your straps are dark or don't blend with your skin tone, they will show through the lace. I wore a beige with straps so got to have the support of a regular bra without them showing through. Overall, I'm so pleased! Best purchase in a while! And I think this one is versatile enough that I could wear it again for a wedding or nice dinner just by getting fancier with the accessories.

Good quality and true to color I'm 5'9", 240lbs and wear a 40H bra. Bought a size 22 and it fits very nicely. Great Price, Great Cut, Great Fit I'm 5'9", 280lbs, with an extreme apple shape body (skinny legs, skinny arms, really large midsection). This dress comes directly to my knee (so appropriate for work) and cuts in at my waist and flares at my tummy. I was super nervous about buying this dress because I was worried about sizing. I wear a 22-24 in most clothes and ordered a 24 in this dress in the green color. It fits like a charm. I probably could have gotten a 22, but the 24 is super comfy and still flattering. The green is more of a teal color than an emerald or hunter green, but it's a beautiful color. I'll be ordering at least the black one, maybe the navy and red as well. If you're like me and you're worried about ordering it, pull the trigger.

Okay. See description for in depth issues/feedback I’m 5ft 5.5in tall 125-130lbs (probably 128 right now). 34D bust usually size small tops and dresses in juniors and XS in women’s and women’s petite sections. I have a longer torso and have a slender frame. I’m not muscled but have an athletic build except for a small pooch in the tummy bellybutton area. The dress is beautiful. I didn’t notice any transparency issues in the medium grey color. It has a white belt on the grey dress that is holes in the grey lace with a piece of white/ivory/cream? Fabric stitched behind the holes on the inside of the dress. This white-ish part is the only slightly see-through portion. The belted area is a bit high up and does in fact rest just below and minorly intruding on the bust. The sideways stitching near the bust is in fact awkward and confusing and unnecessary. I thought it would be less noticeable or smaller in a smaller size dress but that was not the case for the small s 6 or the extra small xs 4. The belted area is also much tighter that the rest of the dress in the S6 and XS4. I detail read reviews to figure out my size and I went against my best judgement and got the Small size 6, based on similar other measurements liking the way smalls for them. NO. WRONG. The pics attached show what it looks like in the armpits in the small 6. The rest of the dress fit well in the 6, but I couldn’t handle all the gappiness and seeing my undergarments so I exchanged it for a xs 4 and it fit great perfectly in the bust but a little tight in the belted high waist which was surprising. I could have worn it it wasn’t that bad but if you don’t like form fitting and minorly snug items, this isn’t for you. I may get a xs 4 in a different color but didn’t need 2 of the same grey colored/styled dresses (I ordered a xs4 grey 3/4sleeve dress with neck keyhole from same brand/seller. That one wasn’t so tight in the waist and fit flawlessly. I did find the keyhole irrelevant and pointless but I did get many compliments on the dress and the keyhole in neckline. The keyhole also was up high enough to be 100% modest which is important to me especially for bending down and with a little one.) The dress came down to my knees and felt nice and comfortable. The fabric had a textured look but was not uncomfortable or awkward. The material and length was the same for the tank and the 3/4 sleeve dresses by same seller. One other review had said the material was like a thrift store 80’s style which made me nervous but I find that completely false. It felt like a textured cotton blend and I am iffy about weird textures. - size xs for best - grey color was nice - zipper had no issues - modest top and to the knees - not see through - tighter and higher up in the waist of the tank lace dress style - more space in the waist of the 3/4 sleeve keyhole dress

Plus Size Girl who struggled with chest area I'm a 5'5 225lb woman with a hefty chest. I got this dress an XX-Large and you can tell it was still pretty snug. I was obsessed with the color and the pattern so I knew I had to make this work! If you know these kinds of dresses it is already super hard to have the fabric lay on your chest without it looking or feeling awkward. I had to play with it a lot and often walking around it would slip and with that, my chest would sag and I got worried about being exposed. I was a little nuts and did it with no bra as I didn't have a strapless bra 😅 If you wear this dress I highly recommend using double-sided tape to avoid it lifting or exposing. Despite all of that, the reason I still rate it highly is that I would not stop receiving compliments! I felt like lil miss popular in it receiving so many compliments on my outfit.

Blue is beautiful but fit is off, runs small I'm a big fan of wrap dresses, so I was excited when I saw this. It unfortunately, though, is not as flattering as most wrap dresses. I ordered a 6, which is their smallest size (I am typically a 4 in dresses). The 6 just fits me, so I would say these run a bit small, order up if you're between sizes. The zipper doesn't fully unzip, so it takes some effort to actually get into the dress! The top part of the dress is very uncomfortable, it pulls on the shoulder that has the strap. Also, it's good to know that the dress is a little on the short side. I'm 5'3" and the size 6 was hitting me mid thigh. If I were any taller, I think it would be too short, unless I was 20 yrs old of course :). Positives are that the color blue is beautiful, the material is nice, and it's lined. Otherwise, it's a pass for me.

Flattering Sweater Dress I'm a BIG girl (210lb, 5'2") and not wearing any shapewear in this pic, and I still feel like this dress is somewhat flattering. It doesn't have any "support" meaning it doesn't lift the girls, or tuck the tummy, or hide the love handles, etc. But it just fit well and doesn't hug too tightly to anything. With some shapewear and cute boots, this is going to be the cutest dress I've worn in a long time. The buttons are super decorative and takes the look of this dress up a notch. The top buttons have button holes so they can be unbuttoned if you need to for any reason, but I didn't need to. This slid over my head without any issues and didn't stretch the neck. The pocket flaps are only flaps, there's no actual pockets. I got the army green in XL and normally wear an XL - I feel like this might run a tad bit on the big side but not enough to size down - just enough so that if you want a tighter fit, you might want to size down. Ohh...and this can be washed AND DRIED! Most sweater dresses have to be laid flat to dry so I was excited when I can just throw this in the dryer and call it a day.

Came stained with blue ink?? I'm giving 3 stars because the dress came stained with dots of blue ink... the dress itself is nice foe the price. For reference I'm 5'4" and weight 147lbs I ordered a medium and it fit perfectly! The dress has stretch in the zipper area which is nice if you're a bit cheat heavy. The material is thick but light weight. Overall it's a good little dress. HOWEVER I tried it on today for our upcoming family pictures and noticed small blotches on the chest area. I thought maybe they were fuzziness. On further inspection they are what appear to be blue ink stains. The dress color I chose is champagne. It's very close to kahki in color so the stains jump out at you. I'm hoping to be able to wash them out but honestly I shouldn't have to spot clean a brand new dress because it's stained... furthermore this may be splashes of dye from the factory which means they're NOT coming out. Hopefully our photographer can edit them out. I would still recommend the dress as a whole but just be wary this may happen when ordering.

Adorable Shirt I’m going to start out saying my measurements typically are 39” bust, 22” waist, 23” torso length, and I typically wear a 36B bra size and am 5’3” tall. I got a large after reading the reviews and looking through the size measurement chart. I was very happy with the results. The material is quite nice and doesn’t wrinkle which is something I love in a shirt (although it does show any moisture so if you have sweating issues or if it’s a rainy day, you should be conscious of that). It’s a beautiful color that I’ve never worn much of, but keep being told I should. I think the original picture was a great representation of the actual color I got. I was worried about the sleeves since it isn’t a very stretchy material and sleeves can often be tight around my upper arms. However, I had no issues and the sleeves are actually a really cute shape. I highly recommend this shirt.

This dress was a hit I’m happy I got this dress for my sisters wedding. Sorry I don’t have a better picture to show. It was comfortable and I was able to go bra free. With large breasts that was a blessing. I received numerous compliments on the dress all day. I thought the corset lace up in the back would help with cinching my waist in but I couldn’t breath and had to have it loosened. Otherwise it was great and for a great price. When ordering I followed the measurement chart to decide what size to order and I was smaller than I expected. I did get a size 14 and at 5’7” I had to have it hemmed only because I was wearing flat sandles. I would absolutely recommend this dress even for a wedding party due to its ability to fit various sizes. It also arrived quickly giving me time to have it altered.
